lines.x: Woo—ha~!
Ya—hoo~!
Hii—yaah~!

There is still a ways to go before we pass through the reed beds and arrive at the Roosting Tree. Shall I take the helm? The boats of the Convallis were carved by incantation, and are powered by the same. They have no need for physical strength to pilot.
It's fine. Those three are busy running wild, let them burn off some of that energy.
They are more... lively than I imagined.
This is them at their calmest, if you can believe it... You don't want to see them rowing while deciding who can cry the loudest, or roar most like a manglerbeast.
They were churned up in that furnace for centuries, after all. They barely had physical form, barely had distinct identities. Anyone would be rowdy after something like that.
...It's quiet, the Convallis.
Pardon?
In Columbia, even a forward-thinking guy like me finds himself flustered at a taxicab door, struggling to find the handle on a newfangled model.
Noisy change is Columbia's natural state. The ever-pioneering, ever-advancing nation. She waits for no man, not even for a second.
But here... When was the last time I came here?
Two hundred years ago, after Kazdel was picked clean in the wake of that bitter war. Væla had only just turned this valley into the home of the Banshees.
Yep. Can't have been easy, finding a place like this snuggled up to all these Originium-doused mountains.

Mist Lake's rippling mirror, sunken trees among the reed beds, forests carpeting the land in gold... and the Banshees working the oars of their boats, gently blowing on their leaf whistles. It's like the valley itself is breathing.
Even after all these years, the Convallis hasn't changed a bit.
Aside from... the one who ferried me the first time around.
Væla.
Væla, my old friend. Did she already move into that wooden house under the Tree?
She did so last month, Tin Man.
I heard she worked you into one of her tales, telling the children that the mists of the Convallis are puffs from your pipe.
But a day later, when the children pestered her as to how you persuaded that empty suit of armor, or escaped being thrown into the Furnace, hearing your name only confused her.
...She's starting to forget.
Indeed. Forgetfulness... Death's warning to a Banshee.
She moved into the house after that, like every other Banshee she has seen off over the years, peacefully waiting for the end to come.
Seeing off an old friend who's forgotten my name... Didn't think my trip here would end up being so heavy.
I would like you to comfort her, in your own way.
She has given far too much of herself for us, for the Convallis, and for the Sarkaz.
You know her type, always insisting on wallowing in unknowable suffering. I simply...
I simply hope that as three hundred years' worth of memories slowly leave her side... She can find some solace.
Sure.
We are here.
Laqeramaline.
Do you require my presence for your goodbyes?
Nobody here.
But... But how?
Væla... left?
Where can a Banshee losing her memory even go?
Whoa! Why's the elevator so dark? I thought there'd be a window or something to look out...
Didn't you read the Propeller Paradise handbook they gave out? It's for building suspense, so everybody here for Air Expo Columbia gets an optimal high-altitude experience!
Advance tickets sold like hotcakes. I tell you what, this is gonna blow those dime-a-dozen rinky-dink drones that've been popping up everywhere the past two years—
Alright, alright, no need to show off how well you memorized the handbook.
We've already put propellers on every piece of furniture in our house that can take them. Does every Columbian these days hear the word 'sky' and get all worked up into a tizzy?
Also, did you notice? Even after all this time, we still haven't reached the levitation layer. That means—
Haha, it means this park really is as tall as the ads say, and our latest levitation tech really can hold up an entire building! We've come to the right place, dear!
Where's that music coming from? Is this... an elevator ad?
Hi everyone! I'm Oakley, your guide to the park!

I get it, being cooped up in that little black box must be awfully boring, you wanna get to the punchline already... but once you get your one-of-a-kind view of all Terra from six hundred meters up, it'll be worth it!
In forty seconds, we'll be arriving at Propeller Paradise's levitation layer. Before that, I'd like you all to noodle on one question: what sort of change will the sky bring to our lives?
You might've already had the chance to try the flying cars in the lower level exhibition area today, trading tires for Originium thrusters.
Soon, the only place you'll see anything with wheels or tracks is gonna be a museum. Doesn't matter if it's a baby stroller or a nomadic city.
Today, we struggle to decide whether to go to Minos or Siesta for summer vacation.
But our indecisionitis is only going to get that much worse once every star in the sky is a potential holiday destination.
The gifts you bring home for your kids after the tour might be a model of the Ring Lab, or maybe a DIY rocket kit.
But it won't be long before an extraterrestrial playmate is the newest preschool trend.
Tonight, we'll be gathered by the radio, betting on whether the station will, out of all the songs about the sky, pick our beloved Good Night, Kristen Wright.
But soon, we'll be forgetting all about that bassy, raspy crooner, because the solar wind will be our new strings, and the galaxy will be our custom-order jukebox.
'The sky is Columbia's next frontier.' That's more than just Vice President Jackson's campaign slogan.
Propeller Paradise proves that every Columbian can be the greatest of Pioneers. There'll always be a place for you somewhere beyond the Starpod.
The Air Expo Columbia begins in just seven days, and with it, everything I just said. Hold onto your tickets, or should I say, your keys to the future.
Everyone's seen the sky opening over Trimounts, of course, and the old adage of 'seeing is believing' has never been more true. So come, see, then believe!

lines.loc: Levitation Layer, Propeller Paradise Experimental Park

lines.x: Nice to finally meet you all. Recognize me? I'm the model for the funny cartoon guide you saw just now, Oakley Parsons.
Welcome to Terra's one-and-only levitating experimental park nomadic plate, Columbia's Propeller Paradise!
Welcome to Propeller Paradise. Your investment is greatly appreciated.
Cloud... Is this restaurant the highest point in the park? Being this close to the sky is really making my heart skip some beats!
What's your drink of choice? Your jaw's been hanging open since the moment you stepped off the elevator, so I imagine you must be mighty thirsty right about now.
How considerate of you, Mr. Tannen. What a fine administrator you are for this Propeller Paradise.

I know just how you feel. I first heard about this park back when I was with the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Arts Units, and Originium. And my mouth went so dry it was like I'd been stuck in the desert for days.

Ever since Vice-President Jackson gave that speech before Congress about Columbia setting off into the sky two years back, everyone's been hoping to be one of the lucky few to reach up and touch the Starpod.
So that's why you gave up the chance for a promotion at the ATAO, to do admin work for Propeller Paradise?
That's right, sir.

Fact of the matter is, I've got a keen eye for potential. Propeller Paradise got D.C.'s backing the moment it started construction, becoming Columbia's only nomadic plate with an autonomous administration.

The brutes at the Department of Defense and those Department of Justice hardheads have no authority here. Even us Bureau folks are only allowed to leave a rulebook on the table. Eighty years of Originium product inspection rights, gone, poof.
As for the non-government agencies, only Maylander's Emergency Management Council has the right to stick around. Fortunately, they only worry about 'unusual accidents' that might happen in the park.

This place isn't even six months old yet, but hundreds of sci-tech companies have set up here, we've got near ten thousand researchers coming and going, and the Intellectual Property and Information Security Office gets almost a hundred patent applications every day.
I have to say, Propeller Paradise's been developing much faster than I expected, and in so short a time.
You're right on the mark. In seven days, the park will be linking up with Max, D.C., officially cutting the ribbon on Air Expo Columbia.
The whole country will be watching the opening ceremony. Every company here is going full throttle on the R&D they're about to show off. No better time to invest than right now.
I've still got a few concerns...
Concern is normal, risks and all that. But as they say, in this park in the sky, fowlbeasts fly right into the pot for you.
I've already poured out the celebratory champagne, so don't let it go to waste. Trust me, it'll slake your thirst... Your thirst for everything.
My God... A-Am I really standing above the clouds? Are those really fowlbeasts flying under my feet? This is crazy, I'm feeling a little...
Light-headed? Thirsty? Heart racing like mad? Sounds like you need some Dr Oxygen.
Right, an oxygen tank...
Wait, this is... a drink?
Stiffer than a stiff drink, softer than a soft drink. Your best friend in the sky, the best choice to beat the summer heat: our park's special brew, Dr Oxygen!
Be honest with me here, weren't you jealous of Sankta wings when you were a kid, even if they can't fly with them?
Well be jealous no more, because drinking Dr Oxygen is like plunging your tongue into liquid wings! It's sweeter than your wildest dreams!
*sigh* What's with the ads? Shouldn't she be giving visitors basic info on the park's upper level?
Just leave her be. Look at the big smiles on the guests. It takes something special to sell ordinary soda like it's some revolutionary invention.
A refreshing soda goes great with the sights and sounds of Propeller Paradise. Okay everyone, follow me, while I introduce the park's levitation layer.
The upper level has eleven main platforms and twenty auxiliary ones. These tube passages are the streets, while flexible cables form the foundations of the platforms.
Just like the structural layer down on the ground, the levitation layer is divided into the experiment zone and the exhibition area.
Each tech firm is allocated a few levitation pods that serve as labs for researchers, or exhibition spaces for the public to try things out for themselves.

The high-altitude elevators only bring you to the lowest of the levitation plates. This ropeway station is the local traffic hub, with more than a hundred 'flight pods' attached to the cables. These cable cars are the only real way to get around.
Want a more exciting way to get around? You could try the emergency jump pads on the support platform. Just don't tell park security I said you could do that.
I will say that pre-expo rules mean you can only test a few of the exhibits right now, but I promise, you're still gonna be feasting.
And as a friendly reminder, the strong sunlight up here will make people thirstier than usual, but there's all sorts of flavors of Dr Oxygen available on every levitation platform.
No need to worry everyone, you're not getting addicted to soda, you're getting addicted to the SKY!
Pleasure doing business with you, Mr. Tannen!
I knew I picked the right man to work with the moment I heard you managed to get the feds to approve this place's special status in only two days!
Pleasure's all mine, sir. Now, if you would please sign here.
Err... That older gentleman's been standing there a while now. He a friend of yours?
Sorry to interrupt your business, just came here looking to reminisce about the good old days.
Well, I'll be. Gustave here was my boss back at the Bureau. When did you show up?
'In this park in the sky, fowlbeasts fly right into the pot for you.'
Haha, that little saying of Mr. Tannen's struck me real deep.
He skipped the best part of the story, though.

Story goes that the fowlbeasts of the Columbian wilds are mighty sly, tough little guys to catch, but the Pioneers of the Great Expansion managed to figure out a way. They'd tie some pretty feathers to their pots and hang up a few dozen of 'em around their camp.

The fowl think it's a potential mate, so they let down their guard, spread their wings, and swoop down... then bam, headfirst into the back of the pot, thinking there's a lovely gal or fella waiting for them even as they meet their maker.
Wait, you mean—
Still spinning tall tales out of that old yarn, Gustave? Last time you said the little beasties were smart enough to tell snare from seduction, and that they never fell for it.
I know you dropped by because you're worried I'd get my tongue tied doing business, but I'm not the same kid I was seven years back. I know how to behave.
Considering the former ATAO chief came over to back me up, are you still concerned, sir?
Hah, seems I was misunderstanding things. Since the both of you gave me your guarantees, I'll just sign here.
Exactly what I wanted to hear. Looking forward to working with you.
Didn't get enough of teaching me back at the Bureau, Chief? Decided to come out of retirement to run me through the same old curriculum in a different classroom?
Now, I never taught you to tell lies on your government applications. That investor's still in the vetting phase, too.
So what? 'Even if Bureau rules tie your hands and mouth, let your tail do the talking.'
You remember my words real well, but where's the results?
Still a third over the target.
Seems to me you'd do better if you untied your hands and mouth, wouldn't you say?
Care to explain?
Take a look at your terminal. You're not just an administrator anymore, you've got total control over Propeller Paradise.
You actually recommended me for chief executive of the entire park?
Managing Propeller Paradise needs a special touch. Don't forget, you're still a Bureau man, so don't make us look bad.
Don't need you to remind me, Chief. We've got everything handled. The expo will go off without a hitch.
Don't get cocky, kid. You and I both know that investor saw through your tricks, even if he signed a huge deal with you.
He won't go back on it. Every company on the Flower Street exchange with the word 'Sky' in its name is guaranteed a sky-high IPO.
Columbia's taking great strides into the air these days. Even little kids who can't spell 'Starpod' play are putting rockets on their folded napkin aircraft.
I'm sure you know where this skymania came from.
Trimounts, two years ago, when a million people witnessed a miracle.
If it wasn't for Kristen Wright, all your talk about progress, the future and conquest wouldn't be worth a dime.
When Rhine Lab first submitted their park project plan to D.C., you were still inspecting butts and tails for Immigration.
There's only one thing at Propeller Paradise I don't control. Still belongs to the one who started the whole thing...
You'd better get a bridle around your risks here. You've only got seven days. What're you going to do?

lines.loc: Ground Layer, Propeller Paradise Experimental Park

lines.x: I need the parameters, Sky.
Sent them over already.
The values for the floating botanical unit have deviated.
This is the first time we're using plants to perform long-distance energy transfer instead of the usual cables, there's still too many unconfirmed hypotheses.
We need to compare the data from this unit with the ones on the upper level if we want quick results. I'll head up for you, Director!
No need. The light sensors on this unit are extremely sensitive to the directionality of the light it's using as an energy source. Errors actually mean it gets a passing grade.
I just told you this two hours ago.
Sorry.
Let me ask again, what sort of tree is planted in the unit you just gave me?
You saw my resume when I interviewed, Director, you know I'm no botanist...
I do.
You're not earning a degree by glancing down at that list of plants you're holding.
You've got something else on your mind.
The advance ticket tour should be over by now. The guide was supposed to bring the visitors down to the lower level exhibitions to see Rhine Lab's vivarium twenty minutes ago.
If we are excluded from the pre-expo, then Rhine Lab's gonna get kicked out of the park when the real expo starts!
Occasionally, visitors to Propeller Paradise notice Rhine's logo plastered on the facilities and talk about the legendary company that performed the miracle of opening up the sky.
But very few of them notice, under the layers of levitating installations, under the very ground itself...
A Rhine Lab director standing beside an unremarkable hole in the dirt.
We don't specialize in attracting visitors, Sky.
Honestly, I forgot what I was good at pretty early into the six months I've been here.

You stuffed a dozen of us researchers into the vivarium after setting up in the park, making us tend to the saplings and debug the botanical units all so the park could have a few fruit trees for decor. No large-scale experimentation or anything.
Do you think we're just here to be gardeners?
No, I know the vivarium is our expo showcase. It's the Ring Lab's legacy, even if it has to stay on the ground...
But how long has it been since we last looked up at the sky?
Nasti Lunorey looks up. Two years ago, deep below the outskirts of Trimounts, she would stand at the bottom of the S.H.A.F.T. and look up at the night sky in much the same way.
It was round, feeble, she could cover it with just one hand, but at the same time she found it captivating and frightening to the mind.
But now, the sky has become altogether unfamiliar.
Tower Mountains Biotech's solar boats, Beachbrella's disk lab, Volvort Kochinski's high-precision astronomical telescope, and Mama John's's all-weather floating billboards...
They crowd the sky with pure, concentrated Columbia.
Rhine proposed the park with only one thing in mind: how to make buildings levitate longer using a structural engineering approach.

The corridors linking the levitating plates, the oxygen and water systems for the experiment zone, this planting unit in my hand... They are important parts of the park's fundamental structure, and Rhine's achievements.

We have only seven days until the expo begins. We need to finish testing and checking all the exhibits as quickly as possible. We're Propeller Paradise's engineering department right now. Don't forget your duty.
That's all? Your dreams, Kristen's dreams in the sky... are just going to die like that?
Sunlight filters through the chaotic tangle of levitating objects before falling onto Nasti's face. She does not reply. She simply pulls down the brim of her hard hat.
(In Sarkaz) Freeze.
The sound of rapid punching.

The incantation draws an invisible wall in midair, blocking every one of the Vouivre's punches. The letters abbreviating her alma mater, the Trimounts Institute of Technology, on her boxing gloves are soaked with sweat.
Finally, the Vouivre stops.
Phew—let's call it a day. Thanks for agreeing to be my practice partner, Nasti.
We've only done half your normal routine.
It's time for you to go. Have you said goodbye to the others?
Not yet. Maryam goes without saying; Muelsyse is in Kjerag, and I don't know the new directors of HR and Structural all that well yet.
As for Justin Jr. and Ferdinand, I think it's better that those two see each other in private...
They can give each other an audience to name drop fancy banquet guests, or rattle off a couple of fanciful theories that are this close to crowning a new king of science.
Besides, it's just a business trip.
There are few things that require a business trip from the head of Engineering personally.
The District has approved our application to consolidate resources for the Horizon Arc Project, and continue developing levitation technology based on the legacy of Galleria Stellaria.
But they have also opened their doors to every tech firm in Columbia, designating an entire plate for the purpose.
Did you see the list of names? Administrators from the ATAO, and even Maylander.
Our proposal is now merely one of thousands. They're already showing as much respect as they could by allowing Rhine Lab to provide foundational engineering support for the plate.
Government officials, tech firm representatives... things will be very busy on the plate. You're not used to dealing with these things.
I haven't heard you complaining about anything... Control.
The Starpod is recovering, but the eyes cast up to the sky linger. When common sense is turned upside-down, what was once ignored has naturally become the center of attention.

Naturally? Since the announcement of the Special Loan Policy, one third of major enterprises have added an aerial operations department, and SMEs are climbing over one anther to jump on the 'sky fever' bandwagon.
I know what you're implying, but I'm not interested in political games.
Even if how things develop is out of our control, Rhine Lab must maintain the initiative as the original proponent, in order to protect what Kristen left behind.
That's not so hard to understand.
Yes. Well, thanks for helping out.
One more thing. Why did you hang a sign that says 'Components Control Section' outside your old office, instead of moving into Kristen's old room?
I'm used to this place.
Muelsyse asked the same question. She grabbed my arm and asked with watery eyes whether I made this choice to honor Kristen, to ensure there would always be a place for Kristen at the company.
I suppose you just feel more comfortable in your old place. You personally oversaw every little detail here, after all.
You were watching over my shoulder the whole time I was furnishing your office. You were even more OCD than Parvis was.
Rhine Lab is the same as it ever was.
I wouldn't quite say that. At least staff who come looking for the Control will actually find her once in a while.
...I'm not sure you're the right person to say that.
Is this why you chose me for the trip?
Make a cut here. Yes, that's the right angle. That way, the branches of the main trunk will not be affected.
Test passed. Give it a tug for me before we pave it, Sky.
Next set. Light sensor performance, normal. Levitation attitude, stable. Energy input and output, check. This needs a tug, too.
Oh, and get the disassembly set from the toolbox for me as well.
Do you still need the high-speed screwdriver and multi-function wrench, then?
Depends... huh? What happened to your voice?
I would love to help, but I have a scissor hanging from my buttons, and I'm holding five... trees? I really can't take any more.
Sorry, I'll get it. Did Sky leave?
He told you three times that he was going to the levitation layer for inspections. You didn't acknowledge him at all.
You could have given me a heads-up.
The First Rule of Secretaries: Stand to one side and smile politely, only stirring when directed by your boss, and shut your ears when the conversation turns confidential.
I assure you I did not hear any of the figures or nouns that you just discussed, nor would I have understood a word of it.
I remember you now. You're from Administration—Gordon Tannen's secretary.
I'm flattered that you remember me, Director Lunorey.
My name is Mercia. You may call me Merry. Mr. Tannen would like to invite you to lunch.
Do you manage his work inbox?
He received your last sixteen rejections.
Then I'll get straight to the point. I know he's not interested in foundation engineering, and he knows that I have no interest in 'proposals' outside of work.
Hm...
Speaking of lunch, though...
Have you changed your mind?
It is, indeed, time for lunch.
Nasti flips through her toolbox, taking out a rectangular object wrapped in foil from underneath all the instruments and neatly-packed toolboxes.
Mercia's curiosity brings her closer. She wants to know what could make this enigmatic director of Rhine Lab decline the most luxurious course at Cloud, the park's fine dining establishment.
A crack appears in her impeccable secretarial smile, however, as she watches Nasti impassively unwrap the object.
Is this... brick... your lunch?
(Mumbles) Ration bar.
What?
Mercia cannot look away from the 'brick'.
Are you hungry? Would you like a bite?
I... I suppose. Thank you...
(Chews with great difficulty)
(Swallows arduously)
*cough* *cough*!
You didn't have to say yes.
The Second Rule of Secretaries: Never decline an offer from your boss's guest, for even the smallest trifle might sour a relationship.
How many rules are there?
Mr. Tannen gave me a booklet that is two centimeters thick.
However, the most important one, and the one that I memorized first, was—
Do whatever it takes to carry out your orders, for no one will listen to your excuses.
Don't force yourself to eat it.
Thank goodness... I mean, thank you for the hospitality.
I have a rule of my own, although it's a bit simpler.
What is it?
I was born in Kazdel. The first rule I learned there is that food is precious, and no one may lie before it.
You're holding my lunch, Mercia, so kindly tell me the truth.
What exactly does Mr. Tannen want to 'discuss' with me?
Reporting in! The core concept of this project is a perfect combination of zero-G simulation and wine tasting by substituting gravity with centrifugal force. 'You may spin, but your wine won't!'
Come to the Lucky Cystybeast floating revolving bar, where it's not just the wine that makes your head spin—
No need to read me the marketing copy. I'm just here to assess and ask questions from an engineering perspective.
Where's the other tester? Any feedback after the rectifications?
*Vomit*...
He didn't sleep well last night! We passed the re-check, didn't we, Mr. Jagger? We won't have our permit revoked?
The Lucky Cystybeast's floating revolving bar does not meet requirements, whether based on engineering reports or tester feedback.
We would love to solve the technical issue of unstable power supply, but we need time...
You had three days, in accordance with the rules of Propeller Paradise.
But it takes at least five days for the developers to discuss a solution!
Propeller Paradise has as many corporations as there are cars on the crossroads of Trimounts Square. Every driver has somewhere to be, but no one pleads to the traffic lights.
There must be another way... I know! We hired close to a hundred testers. We could fire them and hire more researchers with the money!
But you promised us yesterday—
Shut up!
I've reviewed the registration details for Lucky Cystybeast.
You were a grocer in a small town. You lack the professional knowledge for levitation technology research, even if the banks have relaxed the loan criteria for businesses in the aerial sector.
We stand upon Terra's first and only floating plate. Everything here must be worthy of it.
Please, don't tear off that ticket, I put my life savings into the Lucky Cystybeast, please...
That's it?
I'll be back to cleaning Trimounts office building windows in a couple of days?
*Vomit*...
Go on, barf your heart out. At least we got a taste of life in the sky... you won't have anything to throw up when you get back to the frontier, back to the cold and hunger.
Skip the technical inspections before the opening? Absolutely not.

Everyone on Propeller Paradise is racing against the clock to get across the finish line. Mr. Tannen believes that any delay to projects caused by inspections will compromise Propeller Paradise's opening.

In addition, reducing the amount of communication you have to conduct with other tech firms will allow you to devote more of your time to your Rhine Lab research. Mr. Tannen has your best interests at heart.
My best interests?
I spent years wandering the frontier when I first came to Columbia, building houses for the Pioneer Teams and killing bandits for the caravans. Life was hard until I finally got my big break.
If I had someone like Gordon Tannen with my best interests at heart, handling everything I wasn't comfortable with...
That would have been very fortunate, I'm sure.
I would have died out there in the wilderness, I'm sure.
Besides, what he really wants isn't for Rhine to forfeit our control over technical inspections.
How do you know?
You imitate his negotiating style too well. The traps and pitfalls are obvious.
I'm not a Cautus. I'm not in the habit of diving into holes.
Spit it out. What does he really want?
You are as astute and decisive as he said you were.
In that case, I'm sure you understand that, while Mr. Tannen is in charge of Propeller Paradise's overall administration, it is Rhine that has a stranglehold on public opinion.

He doesn't need any accidents at such a critical juncture, nor can he afford one. Therefore, he has put together a list based on project progress, and asks that you perform the inspections in this order.
This way, at least the inspections will have a minimal impact on the progress of individual projects, and minimize the chance of conflict, setting aside the issue of engineering control.
The less hassle for you, the less hassle for him.
I won't try to speculate about Tannen's intentions with the order. We have a compromise.
Give me the list.
Thank you for your understanding, Director Lunorey. I won't trouble you any longer.
One last question. Which rule were you following in the negotiation just now?
The twelfth: Know your boss, and communicate his intentions with absolute faithfulness when negotiating, for you are his personal representative.
However—
Yes?
Before I came, Mr. Tannen stressed how difficult the head of Rhine Lab's Engineering Section was.
I packed blankets because I thought I might have to spend the night in front of your office.
But that's not what I see. You don't need concern from other people, and you're used to cutting through the problem in the most direct possible way. But you still care about people.
Which is why I now have the time to enjoy my lunch in peace, something that I have not done in a long time.
Thank you.
Andrew! Did they hit you again?
Call me Sky.
But you gave yourself that name. Mom and Dad said...
Don't bring them into this! What have they ever done for us, except use us as punching bags? They don't get to decide my name!
I swept floors for six years at TIT and eavesdropped on every course so that I wouldn't turn out like those two piles of garbage!
But...
And you! Don't you have anything better to do than put nagging notes on my bag? It's not like I don't know how to eat—
Those people are here again...
Frickin' hell... they just won't quit, will they? Stay behind me.
You think you're all high n' mighty wearin' that white coat, eh? You and that li'l bitch there will always be slum dregs, no matter what you wear.
Hah... you're a musbeast in a ditch. You wouldn't know greatness if it shat on your face.
Say that again, I dare you!
Andrew!
Wh-What's that?
Catastrophe?! Run!
Catastrophe my ass! It's called exploration! Progress!
The young man, all bruised up, looks up at the sky. He forgets that he is in a dark and dirty alley, with a scared little girl behind him.
He cannot help but go after that ray of light in the sky.
Aren't you worried about the Control?
Like she ever needs us to worry.
We're going the same way. And that's all we are.
We can only play our own parts, and then... pray she's lucky.
(In Sarkaz) If one day, Kazdel too hangs from the firmament, then I'll engrave your name into my stoves.
*pant*... *pant*...
E-Excuse me, ma'am...
Can you tell me about the one who went into the sky...?
Who was that?
Kristen.
Her name was Kristen Wright.
Lucky Cystybeast Food and Beverage will cancel their registration tomorrow, Director.
They didn't pass the re-check?
Not even close. They were one of the first corporations that made the transformation to get into Propeller Paradise, riding on 'sky fever', when review criteria were less strict—
What will happen to the testers they hired?
Their boss can't tell a motor from a transformer. I bet he just saw the name 'Propeller Paradise' as an opportunity to get rich quick...
What were you asking?
Nothing. You seem to like the name.
I wish they would just call it Galleria Stellaria.
I know you didn't approve of publicizing the Ring Lab's technological legacy.
I have no intention of criticizing the Acting Control's decision, but overly conservative decision-making will only cause us to lose the respect we deserve.

Countless tech firms have appeared out of nowhere, based on nothing but speckles of data. The Ring Lab that shone upon millions of Columbians is now educational toys accessible to anyone above the age of five...
'Space' and 'universe' are the buzzwords on every Columbian's lips, but few remember that they came from Kristen's experiment logs that were made public.
No one is truly commemorating her—not even you, Director Lunorey.
Recording and retrospection are historians' work. Our job is to analyze validated data and follow a path that is effective and proven.
And what has come of this data and path?
Over the past couple of years, Rhine has acquired a dozen smaller labs, making progress in projects large and small—with the notable exception of Kristen's legacy.

At this exhibition, we will publish Rhine's subsequent research into the Galleria Stellaria for the first time, putting second-rate tech firms in their place, returning to the realm of aero-exploration and the public eye.

I know the Vivarium was built on the data transmitted down from the Ring Lab, and that the development of a plant-based energy transmission system represents a monumental milestone in engineering design.

Nurturing sprouts, paving botanical units, technical inspections... all these are for the sake of progressing our experiments. But is a 'vivarium' really enough, when it did not even get its day in the sun from the pre-expo?
Please, tell me, Director Lunorey. Rhine has bigger goals, don't we? Like how no one knew that Kristen was going to pierce the sky before the night she did it?
That night over Trimounts was a long time ago.
Get to inspecting the next company. Take note of the change in order.
Yes, ma'am.
Sky.
I know how you feel, and I know that Kristen was the one who inspired you to join us.
Many people looked up at the sky that night. Not everyone chose to chase it.
Don't forget what made you take the first step.
...I'll prepare the inspection documents.
Here's your pass, agreement form and business directory. Once again, I welcome you and your team on behalf of Propeller Paradise!
Next! Can I help you?
I'm cancelling my business registration...
What is the name of the business?
Lucky Cystybeast Food and Beverage.
You will be dismissing eighty-two testers, correct?
Yes, I have their files and my own here.
Thank you. One moment please.
Did the Shadow-Moon fall on my head or something? Why was I so sure I would make my money back that I hired eighty-two amateurs with no corporate experience?
Every company does the same thing. Why does it have to be Lucky Cystybeast that gets the axe?
A million gold certificates down the drain just because we were a little bit slow dealing with problems? You gotta be freaking kidding me!
How the crap did One-Boot Shadow-Moon Real Estate solve that stupid '3D levitating boundary marker' technical issue? That bunch of cobblers?
Your request has been processed, Mr. Aspen. The Propeller Paradise hotline will remain available until the end of the day.
Okay...
However, if you have certain 'special' needs, like One-Boot Shadow-Moon Real Estate did... we can help you with that.
What do you mean?
The smiling staff member takes out the business directory that virtually everyone in Propeller Paradise has a copy of, and points to a phone number.
No name, no address, just an unremarkable phone number hidden among thousands, as tiny as Aspen's hope.
One phone call to this number is gonna solve all my problems? Lucky Cystybeast won't have to leave Propeller Paradise?
Correct.
Then I want to withdraw the terminations and the cancellation of registration—
No need to worry. The registration of Lucky Cystybeast has not been cancelled. However, you do not need to withdraw the termination of your testers.
This phone call will 'completely' solve your problems, and you won't need those people anymore.
A miracle, or a scam...?
Screw it. Anything to stay up here.
Mr. Tannen.
Let me guess. Nasti was skeptical, but she accepted the list and agreed to the proposal?
Yes. I checked the Engineering Department's inspection schedule for today, and it's exactly in the order that you proposed.
But how did you know?
I didn't know what she was going to choose. All I know was that I'm at the apex of my life right now, and everything is going my way!
You're in good spirits.
It's gonna take more than a Rhine director to move the needle.
Open the letter on the table.
'From: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Arts Units, and Originium, To: Mr. Gordon Tannen. Congratulations on your appointment as Chief Executive of Propeller Paradise...'
Two pages? I've never seen a congratulation letter this long.
The reassignment letter that I got six months ago on the border was two lines.
Everyone thinks the ATAO is a sweet gig, but I didn't get to embezzle shit from the savages in Sami or the tribals in the Sargonian rainforest.
The old fox hated me, and the rest of the ATAO turned their noses up at me. Now? He's retired, and I'm promoted. Things sure can change in a hurry, don't they?
It's still light out, but a happy occasion like this calls for a—
A toast.
Your champagne, sir.
Well done, Merry. You've made some progress in your time with me, whether we're talking about Nasti or this glass. Pour one for yourself.
Thank you, sir. However, the Rules are quite clear about not drinking on the job.
Beastshit. I pulled that manual out of my ass.
In that case... would you like to say a few words?
Yeah... the exhibition six days from now is just the start. My opening address will be the toast of all of Columbia.
Every rocket will have my face on it, and the largest district in Shadow-Moon will be named after me.
Rhine, ATAO, the District... no one's ever gonna tell Gordon Tannen what to do! This'll be my Propeller Paradise, forever and ever!
Bottoms-up!
Who are you? Here for the party?
Hello. Are you the Chief Executive of Propeller Paradise?
Oakley, newly appointed guide for Propeller Paradise. I'm supposed to report to a Mr. Gordon Tannen.
Pffft—!
Gordon Tannen spits the champagne out of his mouth.
Shit, bro, take my name if you want, but you didn't have to steal my entire past!
How dare you slander me! I wrote the entire application myself!
Can you recite it from memory?
H-How am I supposed to pull something I wrote a year ago from memory?

'Oakley, born in Bunkerhill, graduate of Trimounts College of Arts and Science, previously worked as a guide in the Human Development and Technological Inovation Department of the District Convention Center...'
'Innovation' is misspelled.
You! Damn you, if I didn't lose my terminal and CV—

lines.st: Gordon's head has never hurt so badly.
The holes in the onboarding process are obvious upon further inspection. But the real question is—
Why would she impersonate a guide?
Is she a Leithanian spy? A DOD agent? Or a pair of eyes that D.C. is keeping on him?
The impostor has already made contact with many corporations at the preview exhibition. Dealing with her now means broadcasting his negligence to the whole country.
Besides, Gordon has no idea how much she knows, and whether she has ill intentions towards him and Columbia...

lines.x: Gordon does not dare to think about it any further.
Your gaze wanders when you speak. Your voice is trembling, and you talk without rhyme or reason.
I doubt Mr. Tannen would want such an 'Oakley' to handle promotion work.
Don't bring Mr. Tannen into this! I tell you—
Shut up!
Perhaps you need a little time to calm down, 'Mister' Oakley. Come with me to the reception room to rest for a while.
Mr. Tannen, I...
Listen up. I just pulled your activity records from the park here. It won't be hard to figure out just who you are.
One more lie, and I'm throwing you off this plate. It won't matter who you are when you're a puddle on the ground six hundred meters down. You get me?
Now talk. Who are you?
...A guide.
You got balls, I'll give you that.
Th-The kind of guide the tourists need!
My name is Ferne. I-I'm a tour guide.
My apologies, Mr. Oakley. Mr. Tannen will make sure that this matter is dealt with promptly.
Do you believe me now?
Of course. You can reach me through this terminal if you need anything. Now, get some rest. I'll be going.
Alright.
Done.
Read the room and avert potential risks for your boss...
Another rule for secretaries.
You said Oakley got himself drunk a month ago after a breakup, and he gave you his resume instead of his contact info, which gave you a chance to sneak into Propeller Paradise under his identity?
That's right.
Your client was an old Banshee who somehow got lost in the Bleached Wasteland. Her memory was hazy and it was difficult to communicate with her?
Yes.
You suspected that she was ill, and you were asking around under the guise of a guide to make her journey a smooth one, which is why you looked like you were up to something?
Exactly!
You've spent a whole month here. You know this is no ordinary place. If you're a foreign spy, the military take you in for questioning, torture, and you'll never again see the light of day.
If you're an agent from the Department of Defense, D.C. won't let you off the hook. They'll disavow, distort, and even dispose of you, all for the sake of tipping the scales to their side.
If you're a District plant, then you've learned too much, and ATAO taught me plenty of ways to help you 'forget'.
But you're just a tour guide who cares for her client. You're a good person.
And Columbia will do good by a good person.
You aren't going to press charges?
Believe me, I'd love to, but too many corporations have noticed your performance at the pre-expo. You'll deal with your problems and get lost before they find out who you are.
The longer you stay, the more I have to compensate the poor guy whose name you stole. If all you want to do is to clear the mind of your Banshee client, I can help.
You know about Banshees?
I know how to solve your problem.
Floating botanical unit connected.
Bioelectrical signal detected. Charging drive unit.
Phew... that's the smoothest these experiments have gone, isn't it?
No, this voice... the short-circuit protection has kicked in.
(In Sarkaz) Unravel.
(In Sarkaz) Unravel!
Hmph.
You shouldn't touch an overloaded converter with your bare hands, Director!
Works better than an incantation. How are the readings?
By exhibition standards, the technology for high-altitude energy transmission through plants is sufficiently mature. By the standards of your expectations for the project, however...
Not good enough. The energy transmission threshold for the floating botanical unit falls short of theoretical values.
That prickly Forte kid would chill out a bit if you said as much to him...
Sky is inexperienced, but he was right about one thing—this Vivarium is Kristen's legacy, and we owe it to her to do our best.
You were always stubborn, going back to when we dug out the S.H.A.F.T. together five years ago.
You pulled all-nighters to ensure its structural integrity, enchanting the core materials with incantations. Nothing could get you to take a break, not even your favorite gummy worms.
If you want to go fincatching, do it at Lake Sulligan. Assuming I approve your leave request, of course.

Besides, I worked so many hours just because I was inefficient. I learned to write curses too late. My incantations suffer in strength and precision compared to my kindred, because they rely on sound as the sole medium.
Is that why you modified your bone pen and use hole-punched paper tapes to supplement your casting?
Yes. I took a lot of wrong turns.
Nasti takes out her bone pen, a plain, unornamented object resembling Rhine's technological creations.
She inserts it into a sapling in the Vivarium, and a white glow rises from the roots, through the branches and into every leaf.
It is as though a command has been entered into the whole Vivarium; terminals begin to execute programs, automatically recording plant energy conversion data.
Such an efficient programming language, your incantations...
Then, we should make more efficient decisions at this bottleneck, despite the risks.
Do you mean...?
Review the report again. Thank you for your work.
Yes, ma'am.
Hello, this is the Rhine Vivarium Lab.
Hello, Director Lunorey. Mr. Tannen has asked me to express his gratitude at your reasonable accommodation that led to successful cooperation the last time.
There are some follow-up matters to be discussed. Do you have time this afternoon?
Yes.
Excellent. I will see you in the Vivarium in an hour.
Ride the ropeway to Flying Donut station, then take the ring corridor...
I'm in the right place, aren't I? It's dark in here. Where's the light...?
Good heavens... wh-where am I?!
Intruder alert, intruder alert.
Activating bio-scan. Analyzing results. Species: Terran. Sex: Male. Threat level: Pending.
A-A talking finbeast?
Mind your words, foolish Terran! I am Space Fin 054. State your Universe ID number, immediately!
Universe ID...?
(Whisper) Your corporate name, nitwit!
Oh, uh, Lucky Cystybeast Food and Beverage.
Ah, so you are the partner that 'Sky Conqueror' was expecting. How unfortunate that I won't get the chance to test my laser cannons.
(Whisper) Don't touch anything. The supervisor will be here momentarily.
Supervisor?
Five-Four, make sure you speak like an extraterrestrial entity next time you do the client explanation thing.
Aye aye, sir.
M-Mr. Tannen?
Shh—don't huff and puff. The polite thing to do when you stand in this collection of unique curiosities, the likes of which you will not find anywhere else in Terra, is to admire it with your eyes.

Look at the wall on your right. Five years ago, I confiscated the possessions of a pioneer trying to illegally cross the border, and found an unusual water circulation and filtration system in his trailer.
He helped me find the core component in exchange for looking the other way. When the unit was assembled, I identified it as a Locus of Sorcery—a Sarkaz witchcraft weapon.
My goodness, it must be dangerous...

Turn around and look behind you. This is a trident Arts unit from Leithanien, created by Witch King cultists who wanted to amplify Arts output so they could rip apart the fabric of reality and summon their master.
Kinda crazy, sure, but its conversion efficiency is fifty times the max allowed by Propeller Paradise regulations. Even an onion could fly with it.
Wait, so you solved One-Boot's technical issues with... contraband?
Contraband? ATAO made up the stupid rule about only using parts made in Columbia!
I don't know and I don't care about the reasons behind the decisions. All I know is that these rules are un-Columbian. They restrict scientists, and force entrepreneurs to lie!
It was a single humble ATAO officer who imported inventions from across Terra into Propeller Paradise, forming the heart and soul of one-third of all levitating facilities here.
One-third... you've run hundreds of smuggling operations without getting exposed?
Every transaction, every deal has gone without a hitch under my management. Not even my secretary Merry knows my secrets.
I... I need time to think about this, Mr. Tannen...
Lucky Cystybeast has one day left, and I got other things on my plate. I'll give you five seconds.
Lucky Cystybeast gets a mongrel heart, or you let it die to your stubborn pride. Choose.
Ah, is this Director Lunorey, Rhine Lab's youngest director ever, Dr. Wright's former right-hand woman, and supervisor of the Vivarium and Propeller Paradise's Engineering Department?
...Is this the 'follow-up' matter that you were talking about, Mercia?

Well, yes. A guide can improve communication in the Engineering Department, and promotional efforts for the Rhine Vivarium, avoiding another frosty reception for Rhine, like what happened at the preview.
I hear the botanical units floating above Propeller Paradise get their lift from incantations, rather than propellers?
Not entirely. Incantations that do not use sound as a medium need to be combined with machinery to maintain operation.
Your bone pen must be extremely important to you. Would losing it have negative consequences, such as loss of memory or illness?
The bone pen is just a tool. It does not have... physiological effects.
How do you relieve stress and recover your energy outside of work?
Does a full-course horn treatment help you relax? Which brand of horn-care product do you recommend? May I touch your horn to see how well it works—
You're asking questions that have nothing to do with our work. I'll have a researcher take you through the Vivarium, to answer any questions you may have.
But—
There are some things that I need to speak with Mercia here about. Alone.
What is Gordon planning this time?
Why do you ask? Mr. Tannen understands that Rhine's current circumstances may give competitors certain ideas, and he does not wish to see it impact the inspection efforts.
He has the best of intentions.
I thought he already traded his conscience for that fancy suit.
Besides, I have a hard time believing that an official guide would ask such irrelevant questions, unless she had ulterior motives to begin with.
I'm not making any accusations. I know you have to protect your boss. But you know you can tell me the truth.
...I agree it's unusual, but I don't think Mr. Tannen is targeting you.
Explain.
We've only just recently come to an accord. There's no reason for us to put pressure on you a second time, in such a short period of time.
Mr. Tannen is a careful man. He would have told me about it if he had other intentions, if only to avoid accidents. Unless...
Unless he's keeping some kind of secret.
He disappears from the office at certain times during the day, every day. At first, I thought he was going out to talk to potential investors.
But I spoke to an investor a few days ago, who complimented Mr. Tannen on getting a contract signed in a single sitting.
Yet he said his negotiations with this particular investor were rocky and needed more time...
Um... why are you looking at me like that?
You just violated the First Rule of Secretaries.
Oh!
Besides, you're not as tense as you used to be, when going over the clues.
...Is that so?
According to the rules, I should close my ears and mouth and think only about the interests of my boss. But now, I'm not sure what he's thinking.
Were you two talking about me?
I'm glad to see the two of you getting along so well. Merry's a shy girl and I thought she would have trouble making friends outside of work.
Mr. Tannen, I...
Is this part of her rules too?
Haha. You're a helluva joker, you know that?
I hope you're not here for the comedy.
I'm here to apologize.
I know how exhausting it must be, inspecting thousands of exhibits while preparing your own at the same time. I just wanted to do my part to help.
You sent a very professional guide.
It's only natural to be nervous when meeting your hero.
I never knew I had a fan like that.
She must be shy, or maybe—
You were trying to avoid suspicion.
What do you mean?
Bring the guide, Merry. I have a few questions about Director Lunorey that I would like to ask her.
She's gone, sir...
Say what—
Director Lunorey! Nutrition supply to all planters has been cut! But there's no error from the control program, and no sign of hardware damage to the central control computer. It's almost as if...
The command to control them has been revoked.
Yes. The converters that were supposed to supply energy to the planters are now running in reverse. The plants will die in minutes if the commands aren't repaired quickly—
No, they've already begun to wilt.
First the leaves, then the branches, and the trunks... the greenery that covered the lab walls is peeled off in a matter of seconds.
Everyone stops to watch the disaster unfolding before their very eyes. They all know what the shriveled, bony branches signify.
It is not just the trees in desperate straits.
What are you standing there for? Hurry up and move the saplings for the exhibition. Those are our last ones—
Everybody freeze!
What...?

Propeller Paradise Police will take control of the Vivarium, effective immediately, and begin an investigation into this incident. No one may approach the equipment until evidence collection is complete!
Security!
Yes, sir.
Put the Vivarium on lockdown.
Yes, sir.
I'm sorry you lost your experiments, Director Lunorey, but I have to ensure the safety of Propeller Paradise.
Your researchers have to leave the lab until the investigation is complete. I'll get someone else to handle the inspections.
When did those guards show up outside the door? Before the alarm sounded, or after?
I don't know what you're talking about.
The researchers can leave. I won't.
Your timing was a little too perfect, Gordon.
*pant*... *pant*...
Thank goodness... no one's on my tail...
Sorry, Mr. Tannen, I'll have to go back on my promise to meet you in the Vivarium.
I think I found what I need to help the Banshee...

lines.st: Ferne opens up her hand. A white object is lying in her palm.
Ten minutes ago, she was walking through the Vivarium, while thinking about what can help restore an aging Banshee's mind.
It must be something that is very important to a Banshee, something that would awaken her instincts.
The small object inserted into a tree caught Ferne's eye, as though ordained by fate.
The white surface is covered in esoteric symbols, patterns floating around it like a glowing cardiograph, its allure irresistible.
Ferne has seen similar symbols and patterns before. It is a Banshee bone pen.

lines.x: Sorry, Director Lunorey, but I'll have to borrow this for a while. I'll return it as soon as my client has recovered.
Hello! Sorry to keep you cooped up in this room all day. I think I found a way to restore your memories—
...Ma'am?
There is no one in the room. Only the creased sheets show any indication that someone used to be here.
Wind blows into the room through the open window. A black feather that fell from a horn quivers on the windowsill.
She's... gone...
Thank you for the hospitality.
The pleasure is all mine. It's such a joy to see a Banshee these days.
Why is that?
Makes it easier for me to ask for an elegy when me or my family reach the end of the road.
Not much has changed with Kazdel, but few Banshees come anymore.
Our departed kin won't find the myriad souls without your guidance.
I'm sorry.
I'll... do my best.
Just kidding. You came to treat that 'Roosting Tree' of yours, right?
Is this the branch?
Incantation analysis failed to discover the reasons behind its decay.
It is the pillar of the entire Banshee race. A friend recommended you as a Child of Soil and Stone familiar with plants. Please—
Let me see what I can do. I'll get some dirt...
Can you get a pot from the backyard? If you see any fowlbeasts stealing vegetables, just chase them away.
'Disperse.'
The fowlbeasts disperse, but the decrepit vegetable garden is not quiet yet. A vegetable sprout vibrates violently in front of Væla.
Then, a black, branch-like horn emerges from the dust-covered leaves.
'Fowlbeasts stealing vegetables', eh...
Get lost or I'll rip your head off!
You shouldn't yell like that. It's bad for your voice.
No need to be afraid. I would not hurt a child.
The young Banshee peeks out from behind the leaves, hiding a half-eaten piece of vegetable leaf behind her back.
Don't you dare take my food.
If it's 'your' food...
What's that in your hand?
Before Væla can react, the young Banshee has snatched the Roosting Tree branch from her hand, and begins trying to eat it.
Pfft. Just a stupid branch that's been lying in the mud. It stinks.
Are you hungry? Let me see... I made some pastries from the dew-caps that grow along the shore of Mist Lake. It's the right season for them.
Hmph! Safer to scrounge food off the dead. Get away from me!
(Menacing howl)
You're not scared? Even those big mercs are scared of that sound.
That is the sound of the Banshees. Of our clan.
I have not heard this note in the song of the Banshees for a long time. Years ago, the last one who sang it...
You are that child...
You know who taught me the sound?
But I've never met you before.
We don't come to Kazdel much these days. Our home is in a distant valley, a place where trees grow, with fruit hanging from their branches.
It's hard to find food. You gotta be ready for a beating.
But let's say the place you're talking about really exists, where fruit just grows on the trees like that... what's the catch?
Clever girl.
Maybe you should come with me. You belong there. You'll like it.
Even if you don't believe it exists.
Why should I?
Your name is Nasti, isn't it?
Is that... the presence of a Banshee I sense? And... what's that? Wither... decay?
No... Nasti... are you...?
Are you trying to repeat the mistakes of the past?
Most of the Rhine staff have evacuated the Vivarium, sir. Only Nasti and a few researchers are staying behind to organize documents. They don't look happy.
Are you happy? The plan wasn't to ruin the whole Vivarium.
You said the tour guide knew too much about the Banshees. This was a chance to expose her and drag Nasti into it. Kill two fowlbeasts with one stone.
It was a rough plan to begin with. Isn't the Vivarium accident a convenient excuse to stop Rhine's inspections?
Convenient? And where do you think we'll find that frickin' guide?
Uh...
I know Nasti. She would never let something like this happen to the Vivarium just accidentally. Besides, there's no way she would risk Rhine's exhibit, with the exhibition right around the corner.
The guide... she was gone before the alarm sounded!
She must have done something.
It's not enough to just evacuate the researchers. There's a good chance that Nasti's already onto my plan, and is just waiting for evidence... evidence that I can't afford to give her.
Tell your men to find Ferne by sundown.
Yes, sir!
Deep breaths, Ferne... maybe she just went off on her own...
No, no, no, she doesn't even remember why she came to Propeller Paradise, where on Terra could she go?
But if that's not what she did...
Then someone must have taken her.
Who's there?!
Miss.
Wh-What are you doing here?
What are you standing there for? Hurry up and move the saplings for the exhibition. Those are our last ones—
All dead... in five minutes?
Mercia reaches out to touch the withered tree in the middle of the Vivarium. She sees a small hole at eye-level.
A natural cavity... no, a connector?
The withered plants. The tree hole. No equipment malfunction... tell me, what was that 'branch' attached to you, and where did it go?
It was Ferne!
You took that bone pen from the tree in the Vivarium, didn't you?
It's plain, simple, unremarkable. You couldn't have known that it was the key that controls the entire Vivarium. It wasn't your intention to sabotage it.
...Is Mr. Tannen waiting outside with his men?
I came alone, Miss. I don't think the responsibility for what happened to the Vivarium should fall on your shoulders.
What do you mean?

There are some things I still have to look into, but I've suspected that Mr. Tannen has been plotting against Rhine ever since he agreed to help the mysterious Banshee guest for no apparent reason. Either that, or his own private interests are involved...
You suspect your own boss? But he said Columbia would do good by a good person.
He also said that there's nobody easier to deal with than a good person.
Then my client...
There's a row of 'VIP rooms' outside Mr. Tannen's office. You could try looking there.
I hope your client just went out for a walk, I truly do.
Gordon must be behind this!
Altering the list of inspections, then sabotaging the whole Vivarium and putting the Rhine exhibit on lockdown... what he gonna do next, chase us out of Propeller Paradise?
There is no problem with our systems or hardware.
He sent someone to steal your bone pen.
It's not like him.
Why are you trying to use logic to explain the actions of a bandit?
They don't operate under the same rules we do, except when they talk officialese. None of the corporations on Propeller Paradise do.
We're closer to the Barrier than anyone else. We're different.
Countless tech firms fall by the wayside, but the Business Registrar rarely gets any complaints. Why?
Because there is a consensus—in the Sky Age, the higher you stand, the more respect you deserve.
Gordon's interference with our experiments violates Propeller Paradise's consensus. He should...
You don't want to be bound by the rules, but you still want to exploit them.
You're proving that Rhine is not yet ready to reach those heights.
What...?
Stop working and listen up, everybody. This is about today's incident, and how we proceed from here.
Cultivation and I have confirmed that the seventy-two trees waiting to be replanted, three hundred and fifty saplings and more than one thousand ungerminated seeds, are all dead.

The Propeller Paradise Police are now in charge of investigating the incident. We don't know how long that will take, but there is no realistic chance that the Vivarium can be restored before the exhibition.
Senior researchers will have to stay behind and work on the incident report with me. The rest of you can enjoy a little time off. Any questions?
Director Lunorey. You said the Vivarium was our most important project, the cumulation of data sent back by the Ring Lab.
Does this mean that we failed to protect the last legacy of Galleria Stellaria?
Dismissed.
VIP rooms outside the office area... here we are.
Mercia had suspicions, but no proof that Mr. Tannen really... never mind, the most important thing is to find the client!
...Try to run again, and I start breaking bones!
There's someone inside?
For anyone's sake, quit crying and keep quiet until the end of the exhibition!
Ugh, my head hurts. Let me get a smoke.
Please, sir, let me go... I won't say anything about Mr. Tannen...
Oakley? What are you doing here? Open your eyes!
The impostor...
I've got a name. It's Ferne.
Mr. Tannen said he was gonna treat you well and pay you compensation. What's going on...?
Treat me well? He freakin' locked me up!
And none of this would've happened, if you didn't impersonate me!
Hey, you're the one who begged me to take your number and resume that night, tears and snot all over your face. What did you say back then? You were a 'lonely soul looking for love'—
Alright, alright, stop, stop, STOP!
Me and my rotten luck... it's a huge deal, letting unauthorized personnel sneak into a high-security experiment zone. That's why they wanted to shut me up.
But how did YOU get out? I heard the guards say that Mr. Tannen was gonna deal with the troublemaking imposter.
...I see.
Are we dead? I just wanted to see what life would be like in the future, but now it looks like I ain't got no future no more...
You wanna join my tour, Real Oakley?
What?
I got in unnoticed, I can get you out safely.
You can?
Man alive, I went out for a smoke and a whole PERSON just grew out from the floor or what?
You can get in, but you won't get out!
Not gonna let us go in peace, huh?

He knew he could not depend on Mercia, and he was confident he could handle this. But he cannot stop his thick tail from nervously slapping the ground, as though it senses a threat, something beyond his control.
Nasti is gazing at him, indecipherable, seemingly hiding something. Eeriness and anxiety cause Gordon Tannen to tighten his left fist in his pocket.
(I was just supposed to keep an eye out with a water clone hidden in the plants! Nasti never told me the head of Propeller Paradise would be so hard to fool!)
(I'd wear myself out arguing with him all night. Let's do this my way.)
Ahem!
The sound of dripping water, which accompanied the trio their whole way down the tunnel, is suddenly silent. Water droplets break free from the constraints of physics and float in midair.
They feel a coolness at their feet. Only now do they notice that the water on the floor has reached their ankles.
Mr. Tannen! Look! I-Is that still Director Lunorey?
Gordon Tannen cannot believe what he is seeing. Water streams into 'Nasti' from all directions, carrying her higher and higher, looking down at the three invaders.
Gordon Tannen has never seen such effects from an Arts unit. Prudence would certainly be in order, if this was Banshee witchcraft. But there is something that scares him even more...
'Nasti' bends down at him, with a cute expression that the one by the name would never wear.
There really isn't a single fresh thing on Terra, is there? I've heard this pre-written spiel a thousand times.
How about we talk about something else? Like, what's in those two big boxes of yours? Don't tell me they're presents for Rhine Lab? We've still got a ways to go until Christmas, I thought?
...Who are you?
Nasti, the director of Rhine's Engineering Section, whose Vivarium you just ruined a few hours ago. Who else?
She would never talk like... THAT. And why do you look like you're saluting? That's a casting gesture, isn't it?
Heh, you noticed.
You can't fool me. I don't know what your Arts do, but this underground passage belongs to Rhine, and anything that happens will only come back to bite you.
You're not satisfied with just losing the experiments for the exhibit, eh? You want to go to jail with the rest of your people?
Let me remind you of something.
Like you said, this underground passage belongs to Rhine. And you personally gave the order to shut it off.
But I'd bet a whole lot of gold certificates that your people don't know about tonight's 'investigation'.
How many would know where to go to look for your corpses?
You dare...!
...Run!
It is nighttime. Nasti feels a daze as she walks the streets of Propeller Paradise.
This place began as a proposal from Rhine, built by Nasti personally. But Nasti no longer recognizes any of the myriad inventions that adorn it.
She even has to stop and think in order to figure out how the inflatable street lamps by the roadside work.
Nasti cannot help but feel that she is walking into an unfamiliar life.
It's like when I first came to Rhine.
Muelsyse emerged from the sink, teasing me for my ignorance as she walked me through sterile lab cleaning procedures.
Nasti stops in her tracks. Another fragment of the bone pen lies quietly in the gaps of the bricks, having guided her all the way here.
She picks up the fragment, as she once did when she walked into a new life for the first time.
Young Nasti walks the forests of the Convallis, carrying a bag far larger than she is, and stuffing it with broken twigs.
It has been two weeks since she and Væla arrived in the Convallis, and Væla is busy with younger Banshees who come to her with questions of every kind.
Only when the Banshees are all asleep and there is no one in the valley, does Væla have time to take Nasti's hand and walk with her through this beautiful place...
Finally, Nasti decided to build a treehouse on her own. But the bag of twigs is too heavy for her, and she must wait for Væla to pick her up.
It has been so long since the rainy season. Isn't it time for another rowing competition? When do we...?
Let me clear the mud off the eastern shores of the lake.
But no 'spin' incantation on the boat this time, please.
Teacher! One of the mistapple vines is getting in my house. Can I cut it?
No, my child. Nothing is better for wiping one's feather horns, than the pulp of the mistapple. Perhaps you could 'guide' the vine to grow in a different direction?
It'll be a chance for me to see if you've been practicing the incantations I taught you.
How long has it been since we last received a 'token' from outside, Custodian?
We've only had five or six this month. I dealt with them when I was in Kazdel to ask the Child of Soil and Stone to treat the Roosting Tree.
So few?
Isn't it a good thing?
It's been half a year since you taught me the elegies, but I haven't really had the chance to sing for anyone... can I go next time, and help our kin...?
Alright, let Væla rest.
It's all right. This is what I'm supposed to do.
The children call you 'Teacher' and 'Custodian'. I never conferred these titles.
What you've done for the Convallis over the past two centuries puts me to shame as the Lord of the Banshees.
You know the reason. I have to keep working... to distract myself.
Did Duq'arael cause you any trouble during your audience with Her Highness? He's been hostile towards our people ever since we retired to the Convallis.
He's hostile to everyone. But speaking of Her Highness...
She showed me the early fruits of her exploration into the nature of Originium. Perhaps she has already chosen a more resolute path.
What about us, then? What about the Convallis?
The Convallis is no longer associated with the future of the Sarkaz, even if Her Highness really has changed her mind.
It remains the Banshee sanctuary.
You brought a girl back a little while ago, didn't you?
The daughter of a stray sister... I should have found her five years ago.
Aefanyl keeps asking me when he can come and see her... he's so curious about everything beyond the valley.
The boy...
Please tell Aefanyl that Nasti is rather timid and needs time to get used to the Convallis...
That's not true. I'm not timid. You know that.
Yes. But you'll be living with other Banshees for a very long time.
They'll share fruit with you, rather than throw rocks at you. Living with them will be very different from living in Kazdel.
That's why I want your meeting to be more... formal. Give me time to prepare, won't you?
Understood.
But what are you preparing? Does it have something to do with the 'token from outside' that you were talking about?
The things in your house? Those black blood crystals, dirty shrouds and dried-up hearts?
But why do you say there were only five or six?
You always stay up late, peering at them, with a deep frown on your face—
...Nasti.
Do you like this valley?
Yes.
There's no blood in the mud, no black rocks in houses, no fighting, plenty of food, and no one ever disturbs you...
Do you want to stay here?
Yes. That's why I want to build my own treehouse soon.
Have you decided where?
There's a big, beautiful tree deep in the valley, right? What's the name... the Roosting Tree?
Yes. It's the Banshees' final resting place.
Everyone lives there, right? Even though you've never taken me there...
I want to live closer to that place. Can I?
And when will you teach me incantations? That way, I can move this bag of twigs myself.
No need to be so hurried.
When will you teach me how to play the bone whistle? That way, I could play in front of the others when we play together.
I took one from your house, but I couldn't make a sound with it.
No need to be so hurried, my dear girl.
The tired Banshee suddenly seems so unfamiliar to Nasti. She knows where Væla always starts when polishing her own feather horns, and how many nearly indistinguishable black dresses she has.
But she can never tell what Væla is thinking.
Nasti stops asking questions and raises the bone whistle, obstinately blowing on it in futility.
Until a soaring melody covers up the muffled sound she is making.
You want to play the whistle for the other children, don't you?
One does not need a bone whistle to accompany a smile.
Come, child. I'll teach you.

Nasti does not understand how that simple leaf in Væla's hands can make a sound, much less why that sound is so full of sorrow. The sky is blue, the waters of Mist Lake murmur on, and everything seems perfect.
Nasti follows Væla's gaze. The branches of the Roosting Tree are just barely visible in the distance, swaying like reeds—like fate.
Nasti is still in Rhine experiment area, and is preparing to kill Tannen?
Stay behind me, Miss Selene.
I run faster than Mr. Tannen, should we really get into a fight with a Banshee.
But that 'Nasti' was just a potted plant.
A water clone?
Muelsyse? Here?
Just a little gadget enchanted with Water Elf Arts. Doesn't actually do much.
A combination of Nasti's image, Muelsyse's inspiration, and home field advantage... no one could have predicted it. No wonder those three were scared away.
Should we evacuate with Mr. Tannen, then?
No. Keep going, following the traces left behind by the water.
According to the blueprints, we're almost at a dead end.
There's nothing except the water marks. Maybe that was indeed a bluff by Muelsyse.
Nasti is a genius engineer. It wouldn't be hard for her to leave a hidden space out of the blueprints. Besides, the presence of the water clone itself is a sign that there are potential secrets here.
I've been thinking. Why is Nasti the only one here, when there are two Rhine directors working on the project?
By all accounts, the Vivarium should fall within Muelsyse's domain, shouldn't it?
Unless... by its very nature, it's something only Nasti can do.
You mean...
If your potted plant suddenly died—sorry, purely hypothetical—would your first reaction be to keep checking the time on your watch over and over?
No. I would be more concerned about the cause of death than the time.
But that was what the most experienced researchers near Nasti did when the incident at the Vivarium occurred—check the time.
It was almost as though they expected the wilting to happen.
It just didn't happen at the expected time.
Therefore, Rhine's claim that they were studying plants that can survive at high altitude, based on data from the Galleria Stellaria...
It's a cover story.
I think Maylander has found Rhine's true secret.
Rhine doesn't need the plants in the Vivarium to live. It needs them to die.
Not the life that an Elf celebrates, but the death that a Banshee knows well.
But why?
Death will 'water'—
A 'tree' that is even more special.
The elderly Banshee stands in the shadow at the end of the street. She has waited a long time. The night is longer than the three centuries that she has lived.
She watches as the familiar figure approaches...
It is taller and sturdier than she remembers.
The facial features look different. The clothes are of an unfamiliar style.
Not so familiar anymore.
Væla's head begins to ache. Her chaotic memories are like Mist Lake in deep winter, the cold and heavy mist swallowing her in an instant.
Na...
(In Sarkaz) Separate.
(In Sarkaz) Guide.
Nasti places the fragments of the bone pen in the center of her palm, and recites the language of the Banshees.
The incantation separates the scent that does not belong to her, but is very familiar, and leads her to its source.
There is no wind, but the bone pen begins to sway... all will be answered, soon.
Then—
Na... sti... careful...
What's the situation over there?
There's... someone else...
*pant*... *pant*...
M-Mr. Tannen... stop...
I think we're lost.
I think we're lost too. I can't believe how much space Rhine has beneath the experiment area. This has got to be against the rules...
Usually, when scientists leave so much space in their labs, they're either saving it for a big plan... or it's from a big plan that's already gone off the rails.
S-Sir, is it just me, or is the ground...?
The ground's cracking...
...Looks like it's the former.
S-Somebody help...
When did the door behind us close?
Dammit, please tell me it's not a giant missile, Nasti.
The concrete beneath their feet shatters, as though it were made from crackers.
Gordon Tannen tries to hook the pillars with his tail, grab the walls with his hands, but all he finds is... a single twig, glowing dimly.
Beneath him, a canopy made from metal and withered wood expands in the darkness.
The glowing tree rises up silently into the night sky, as if a thousand stars are rushing upwards.
This thing...
Does it have a propeller? An engine?
There isn't any sound of machinery... how could it possibly fly?
Nasti, Rhine Lab... I'll be completely damned.
Shit, why do these guards feel the need to shine lights into the sky just because some humongous thing is taking off in the middle of the night? Who the hell trained them—oh, right, it was me. Great.
Would you say we're... exposed, sir?
I would say we are, given that everyone who's awake in Propeller Paradise right now is seeing us hanging off a tree with two big boxes of contraband.
You think there's a chance if I yelled that I found some dangerous Rhine experiments to the people below?
...No, no, no. Not a finkin' chance. I can't let this thing get ruined, until I figure out how on Terra it could fly.
Okay, it really is over now.
Wh-What just happened?
I think our intrusion might have awakened something...
But now, there are no secrets on Propeller Paradise anymore.
Is this another miracle, Director Lunorey? Or is it...?
A stillborn miracle?
No need to say anything. I can see it all from here.
...I'll head back right away.
Na...
A moment of hesitation, and the elderly Banshee fails to say the name.
Væla watches as the young Banshee departs. She looks in her direction, and sees a huge, glowing tree, slowly rising into the sky.
The roots of the tree are not secure, swaying in the night breeze. At this distance, they sway like weeds, like fate.
Is that... the Roosting Tree?
What the freak is with that tree up in the air?
There are only a few days until the exhibition! How many stands does that thing take up? Rhine Lab didn't apply for upper-level exhibition rights!
Rhine and the ATAO must be colluding! I saw Gordon Tannen on that tree that night!
Gustave, former ATAO Director, promised in a broadcast that he'd have an explanation the next morning. And what actually happened?
Not one word from the committee! I bet they're stalling for time!
Please remain patient.

The credibility of the Propeller Paradise administration and engineering departments is at stake here. We need to deal with this carefully and come up with a plan that can satisfy the hundreds of corporations that have an interest in the project.
Who's asking you to deal with it carefully?
Yes?
Who gives a crap? It's not like this is another Ring Lab.
The engineering department's inspections have stopped, and the administration's vetting is over. Who cares if Propeller Paradise officials are doing their jobs or not?
But the hearing is a necessary part of the oversight process...
Investigations and trials, those are your problem. We just want to know the result of the hearing—
The Air Expo Columbia we expected to make a splash in Columbia and even all of Terra—is it gonna happen or not?
Out of the way.
I said, out of the way.
You really think that sitting here puts your illegal equipment out of our reach?
It remains Rhine Lab property until the hearing is underway. Besides, what are you calling 'illegal equipment'?
Don't you recognize a floating botanical unit when you see it? The saplings floating around you are no different from the one behind us.
You insolent little... I need more men over here!
Sky, we don't have the numbers. We can only hold out for so long, if they decide to use force.
Besides, we don't know whether this tree-like device behind us is legal, or whether it's safe. After all, it's only been two years since Control Wright...
I'll ask you one question. As a scientist, are you willing to just hand over the product of Director Lunorey's sweat and blood?
No, but...
Then take action. Leave no regrets.
If only we had more people. But Rhine only has so many people on Propeller Paradise...
Did you forget that we're Rhine Lab people too, Mr. Jagger?
I didn't want to drag you into this.
You're worried about the hearing? Hah, it's not like we haven't seen our share of trumped-up charges.
Those guards wouldn't have gotten onto this platform if you called us sooner.
Bunch of slumslugs trying to play the hero?
