Possession of illegal equipment, destruction of property, aiding and abetting... there's enough to keep all of you locked away for years!
I'd already be locked away if Director Lunorey didn't take me in.
You wanna touch the tree, you have to go through us!
Don't threaten me with a good time!
Rhine researchers and testers form a wall against the guards attempting to enter, while curious onlookers on the outside look up at the towering 'tree'.
A tall, dark figure walks past the rear of the crowd, footsteps clanking.
So... this is Rhine's 'tree'.
It's an honor to see you here.
Oh, you flatter me, Mercia. I've been on an extended holiday and it's about time I got back in the saddle.
Are you here for the hearing?
Yes. The design of the Maylander Emergency Management Council badge is pretty nice. You should try one on.
Is my mission over?
Our mission has only just started.
What are your orders?
No rush. These past few days have been quite eventful. Other than Nasti and Gordon Tannen, are there any other stories that I should hear?
Good.
See you at the ropeway station in five minutes. There's so much going on with Propeller Paradise that I'm sure I'll need a local guide.
Understood.
(Broken muttering)
Sorry, I should have closed the window.
Company reps and testers are protesting on the streets. The longer the hearing takes, the more restless they get.
Are you feeling better?
(Weak breathing)
You're in poor shape.
You've already seen Director Lunorey. Perhaps I should send you back to the Convallis—
No... no...
Another no, just like last night.
Things will only get messier on Propeller Paradise in the coming days.
It'll be even more difficult to leave.
We're in jail again! They didn't even bother to change our cell!
The time of the hearing keeps changing... you think they might be looking for more evidence and stack even more charges on us?
Look, if they ask, I'm telling the truth that you're the impostor!
Come on, say something, you're scaring me...
You know, nomadic prisons on the barrenlands could become tourist attractions. The only problem is that it's much easier to get in than out.
You're still thinking about tours at a time like this?
You've already lost one client, and now you're putting your other one in jail?
I'll find her. And there's no need for you to worry.
How do you figure?
No trial, no ID check, no notice to appear... you think the guards are just stalling for time to find a way to charge us?
Yes.
I don't think so. I think it's a sign—
You and I are not the focus of this hearing.
Ferne and Oakley are in jail. Merry hasn't been seen since last night. All the phone lines at the registrar are busy. Everyone and their Originium slugs are looking for me and Nasti.
It's time I put what I learned from the old man into practice. I'll have an answer for everything. I have nothing to fear!
Looks like the old fox has lost his touch. No one's come to interrogate me yet.
Wait a second!
Phew... alright, I still show up as the chief executive of Propeller Paradise on the terminal.
Beep—'Sky Conqueror' is currently under excessive stress. Please remember your health. We are on a long journey that leads beyond Terra.
Dammit, are they gonna start that hearing or not? Everything would be over and done with if I were in charge! That old coot just keeps tripping me up. It's been this way for seven years!
Why haven't you gotten one bit better in the past seven years?
Gustave...
How would YOU do it, if you were in charge?
Uh...
You thought you could pat yourself on the back for stopping the Rhine inspections, thinking you could continue to run your little hustle worry-free...
What do you think ATAO was, a kindergarten? Is that how you ended up this naive?
Everything would have gone smoothly if not for that bloody tree! This is all Nasti's fault!
Smoothly? Even a junior assistant under Nasti saw through your tricks.
The whole country would've seen your dirty laundry if he weren't every bit as naive as you, so much that he came to me for help.
So tell me, how do you plan on dealing with it?
...Please... help me... sir...
Hmph. Whatever the reason Rhine had to build this floating unit, we can't let them continue to define their own creations.
The administration should have had full authority over this, but it has been excluded from the assessment, thanks to your incompetence.
You mean, we'll need a third party?
A scientific consultant who is objective, professional, with the appearance of impartiality.
Mr. Tannen. It's a pleasure.
He doesn't look like a scientific consultant at all... can he really handle this Rhine stuff?
I visited the floating platform in order to familiarize myself with the assessment subject.
It's certainly an impressive 'tree'. And there was plenty of activity in its shade.
Over our cold, dead bodies!
(Mr. Jagger...)
(Just bluffing.)
Did radiation from beyond the Starpod fry your brain or something? You really want to bleed for this?
You'll get your wish, then!
Stop.
That uniform... you're with Maylander?

We are with the Maylander Foundation's Emergency Management Council. According to the bylaws of Propeller Paradise, the Council has the authority to take over all administrative and management functions in the event of an accident or disaster.
You can check with Gordon Tannen and Gustave.
Emergency Management Council?
Yes, Mr. Jagger.
We understand your position, but we advise you to avoid any reckless actions. We are authorized to take extraordinary measures against you and your colleagues, if necessary.
So Propeller Paradise is in a state of emergency, and your preliminary investigation has defined yesterday's event as a—'special incident'?
Yes. The Council has established a special investigation team to obtain evidence from the scene and persons of interest.
The investigation is ongoing. A hearing is the only fair and just course of action, in the absence of an official judicial institution. We thank you for your patience.
Does Rhine keep possession of this 'floating botanical unit' until the hearing?
Of course.
I hope your investigation is as fair and just as the conclusion you just reached.
There is no need to worry.
The safety of the temporary floating platform has been ensured. Bravo is resuming patrol.
Are we just gonna leave, Mr. Jagger?
Maylander's authority looks real, judging from the reaction of the guards.
The hearing keeps getting delayed, and things are still in flux. We won't learn anything if we stay here. Besides...
Have any of you seen Director Lunorey?
A small sandbeast in the forest caught my attention. My knowledge told me that this creature usually makes its habitat in the barrens.
The frightened beast escaped into the bushes. I pursued it through thorn and briar, driven by curiosity, and found myself gazing upon an unexpected landscape...
Do you see it, Fenny?
Yeah!
The great desert and the sky, pink where they meet on the horizon. It's totally different from the pictures in the textbooks.
It smells sweet.
Heh, that's your mom baking cupcakes. They'll be ready soon.
Tell me again about your adventures in the eastern wasteland... no, tell me about the city in the sky!
There's no rush, Fenny. We have plenty of time, and plenty of adventures to talk about.
You'll see everything that I've ever seen, when you grow up and your feet become quicker, I promise.
Stop spinning tall tales to my clients!
Don't blame me for you being boring, trainee. They like my stories better.
My job is to take clients to see places that actually exist.
You think only the places that your company marked exist? What about the things I saw in my youth?
I told you, you shouldn't just read from your tourist manual. You need to walk a path that no one has ever seen—
And achieve nothing in life, get laughed at by the neighbors, and spend my old age spinning tall tales to children?
I believed your stories—the flying beckbeast, floating city, golden-pink deserts... they'll always be precious memories.
But you believe in your stories too much.
Ferne! *cough* Come back here!
Congratulations, Miss Ferne Barn. Your application has been approved. You are now the owner of an independent travel agency.
Thank you. It's a big first step, even if it's a one-woman company!
One woman? I thought there was another name on the application...?
Martin Barn, my dad. Passed away last year.
I'm sorry...
No need to apologize. I wouldn't have decided to start my own travel agency, if not for him.
Even if we won't be able to travel together, Martin Barn will always be an important part of this company.
I'm sure he'd be glad. Have you decided your first route? I can recommend a few popular spots.
Hm... is there someplace that no one's ever gone before?
Most of my peers think my slogan of going into the unknown is just a gimmick. Only my clients and I know how many wonderful sights we saw, and how many amazing things we did together.
Our destination was Propeller Paradise. I wanted to see what the future would be like, while my client had an ambiguous wish.
I swear I didn't mean to sabotage Rhine... my client wasn't in good shape, and you were the only other Banshee I knew of—
No need to apologize. I'm not here to put you on trial. I just wanted to know who you and your client were.
I'll be happy to read your autobiography, if you ever publish one. But right now, time is of the essence.
You're worried about the hearing? I don't think the investigation is complete yet, since they keep pushing the date back.
The trial will come sooner than you think.
Tell me about your Banshee client, as succinctly as you can. When did you meet? Why did you come here? And... how is her health?
It was two months ago, in the Bleached Wasteland...
Brrr... it's really cold at night. The sand's black, completely different from what dad said.
The outskirts of the Bleached Wasteland aren't as scary as the stories say. My flesh hasn't been ripped from my bones, and I haven't heard the ghosts of the dead wailing.
Wait, what's that shadow on the slope? Don't tell me there actually are ghosts here...
Is this... Columbia?
'Guide.'
Gui-*cough*... guide...
Are you alright?!
Pulse and breathing, normal. Doesn't seem like dehydration... well, can't hurt to try.
Am I... back in the Convallis?
No... I haven't seen her yet... do not mourn me... I must not roost...
Calm down. Do you remember your name?
No...
Where are you going?
You're in the Bleached Wasteland, in Columbia. Do you remember what you're here for?
Wasteland...?
No... she's not here...
What are you looking for? Maybe I can help. Ferne's Tours will fulfill your every wish, even the ones you think won't come true, and our prices are fair and reasonable!
My dad always said that if it hasn't come true yet, there's always a chance. He also said... ma'am? Ma'am!
She's lost consciousness. Feather horns... what's this Banshee looking for, and how did she get lost in the Bleached Wasteland?
What are these floating symbols? What language is this? And these golden patterns carrying tiny leaves... is this her Arts?
The leaves are moving... pointing towards D.C.?
Her memory got fuzzier and fuzzier. I didn't know what was wrong with her and I got desperate.
You can't stop time.
It's a symptom of age? But her memories...
I've seen this before in the Convallis. It goes hand-in-hand with death.
If a Banshee who's starting to lose her memory showed up here, then perhaps...
It's her last wish to keep what's left of her mind together.
Do you mean...?
You're trying to fulfill her dying wish.
She came all the way to Columbia, walked through the Bleached Wasteland on foot, just to fulfill her final wish?
What is she looking for? An object, a truth, or someone she cared about?
I see...
Every cop on Propeller Paradise is looking for you. Rhine needs to give everyone an explanation.
This delayed hearing is extremely important to you, yet you came to look for a guide you've only met in passing, asking about her client...
Unless you already know who she's looking for, or—
Is it you she came to Columbia for?
Thank you for caring for my mother, Ferne.
She must still be somewhere on Propeller Paradise! I'll help you look for her!
We're out of time.
Please come with us, Director Lunorey.
A few more minutes, please... wait, your uniform... it's not Tannen who sent you.
The Maylander Emergency Management Council? Your new uniforms look just like the old ones.
The Council has completed its investigation concerning the flight of the illegal device that occurred last night. Please accompany us to verify the findings.
Please communicate any objections that you may have, to avoid unnecessary misunderstandings and waste of time at the hearing.
The investigation is complete... then why hasn't the hearing started? We've been waiting for seven hours!
The hearing is delayed, but the trial has already begun.
I can understand if a scientist doesn't like to talk. Have YOU forgotten how to speak too?
I remember how I was on the shortlist for promotion. The next day, you transferred an official of the same rank over from HQ.
He was young, superb... made all the candidates uneasy. We climbed over each other to prove to you that we were the best one for the job.
Same old story, isn't it?
In the end, you were the one who got the promotion.
I was supposed to get it!
Nothing is ever 'supposed' to happen, kid.
You really think the chief executive of Propeller Paradise is an important post? No, people will only remember the one who gives the opening address.
It doesn't necessarily have to be you.
Gordon has his eyes on the Kuranta behind Gustave. The spikes on the Savra's tail rise up on their ends, swaying conspicuously. He is on guard.
He knows that Gustave is intentionally agitating his competitiveness, but after so many years at ATAO, he just can't stop himself from provoking his competitors.
In order to leave ATAO, he took on tasks that were not his job, pulled strings everywhere, and bowed his head to Gustave, his tormentor.
He must not stop now.
What do you think Rhine's device is, Mr. Consultant?
Mm. Maybe a variation of a floating botanical unit?
Excuses—
No, no. Floating botanical units are electrical cables in the sky. Light weight and mobility are paramount.
What did I see on my way here...? Two empty slots on the tree's internal activator.
My guess is—
Oh, anyone can tell it's waiting for two 'keys'.
It doesn't look like the tree will fully activate until a second Rhine Lab section director is present. Unless someone else sneaks into Propeller Paradise unnoticed...
What do you mean...?
People might have many theories and speculations about this device because of its appearance and the Rhine name, but I see nothing more than a flying 'tree', Mr. Gustave.
Are you done? We're not here to write Rhine's paper for them.
The delayed hearing has bought us time. But the reason Nasti concealed the tree's existence, how Ferne sneaked in, and my side hustle... everything needs an explanation.
Enough. Impatience is a sign of fear, and questions are a sign of incompetence.
Our consultant here has already given us the answer.
What is it?
Rhine's device is pretty low-risk, but also invites all kinds of speculation. Therefore, our definition of it can be as big or as small as we want it to be.
What else do you need me to do, sir?
Do as you're told.
All you need to do is what you're told.
Stop pushing!
P-Please, let me through, I have something to ask the desk...
Don't waste your time. They don't know when the hearing is.
I just want to know if I should come forward to the investigation...
Listen, we're impatient too. No one can do anything to move the hearing process forward, unless you're actually involved in the case.
Uh...
Let me guess. You took out a 'Sky Loan', didn't you?
Yes... there were six grocers like me back home. We spent our days betting drinks on the direction that tumbleweed rolled, until two years ago...
The sky, the Starpod, life free of Originium, a brand-new frontier for Columbia... I didn't understand a thing that the TV and newspapers said, but I saw the light that night.
I sold my shop, abandoned my wife, took out a huge loan, and went into Propeller Paradise without ever looking back.
I've already heard a dozen stories like that today. Take a banner and go join the protest instead of hanging your head here.

lines.st: Aspen looks down at the protest banner in his hand. The fabric is silver, a reflective material commonly used by flying machines.
Lucky Cystybeast's furniture uses the same materials too. Aspen does not understand how it became so popular so quickly. The sofa reflects his face whenever he walks past it. It is patently absurd.
Yet the sight of the silvery fabric also causes his heart to leap, as though the future is really in his hands, and luck is really on his side.

lines.x: I'll make it... I'll make it through this hearing.
Even if I'm put on trial and thrown in jail, I'll make sure the name Lucky Cystybeast and my exhibits stay on Propeller Paradise, where everybody can see them.
Things will turn out fine. Lucky Cystybeast will bring me luck!
He's never been late before... was he delayed by the protests, or did he get lost on the way?
The structure of Propeller Paradise is certainly complex, but it's not so hard to understand if we see it as a massive Wrankwood film set.
I remember the pioneer-era classics that you starred in. People still think your metal body was some kind of special effect.
You have a good memory.
Transportation on the levitating layer is very interesting. The combination of steel wires and ball pods seems like some sort of amusement park.
I apologize for the delay. I had to make a few rounds to humor these three stooges.
I haven't had a chance to greet them yet.

No need to be nervous. Young Maylander agents always attract too much attention. It takes someone with a calmer demeanor like you to play the part of a secretary. But I can't help but feel like you're starting to go too far with this seriousness thing.
Tannen isn't an easy customer, is he?
He has... a personality. It's not hard to read.
Good work.
Your timing a few days ago was impeccable. A single squad eliminated two major risks before the exhibition. I'll be sure to make a note on your annual assessment.
I'm flattered.
The suspension of Propeller Paradise's administrative and engineering functions has created plenty of room for Maylander to operate.
Gustave's desire to work with us is clear. He has already sent people to deal with Rhine's devices, and he intends to personally instruct his subordinates.
I guess all that's left is a hearing to placate the public?
You have questions about the delay of the hearing, don't you?
It's not like Maylander to stall.
Since when did you pick up an impatient streak? Everything happens in due course. We're not only here to investigate.
Such a beautiful, futuristic city. I leave Columbia for a few months, and all sorts of new stuff pop up.
How long will it take to make a full round of the levitation layer?
Less than an hour by ropeway.
Very good. I would like to enjoy a nice view of Propeller Paradise before the hearing.
The wind blows on the leaves in the young Banshee's hand, and voices fall from the breeze.
(In Sarkaz) One more! One more story!
(In Sarkaz) How about the tale of the wandering metal hero?
(In Sarkaz) He even graced our valley once. In fact, the mist that shrouds Mist Lake was actually blown from his big pipe...
(In Sarkaz) Retract your feather horns like this, and you can shield your eyes.
(In Sarkaz) So we can use them as an eye mask to sleep during the day?
(In Sarkaz) No, of course not. This is so you can easily weave your tears into elegies at funerals...
Another recording ends, and the young Banshee keeps her leaves in a chest pocket, yet her gaze suddenly turns wary.
Eavesdrop again, and I'll throw you into the concrete mixer.
Was that... a Banshee's incantation?
Yes.
You've journeyed thousands of miles from Kazdel to Columbia, yet you record your people's voices to keep by your side, listening to them over and over?
Is homesickness hard for you to comprehend?
No. But, it's rare I find someone concentrating on nostalgia the same way she concentrates on a blueprint.
You're trying to decipher the Banshees' language and behavior. Why? To find a new path, to solve your entire race's plight that has endured for millennia?
Gah...
I'm not that arrogant.
I only hope to take a burden off the shoulders of a particularly worried individual.
I see, though that's pretty much what I just said.
So, any breakthroughs?
I'm not in that much of a rush.
It sounds as if you've replayed that countless times over...
Soaking in the same old corpus until you drown, unable to develop a new language.
The limits of vision and thought drown us in the pool of 'common sense', and then everything turns into so-called destiny...
I thought you left Kazdel knowing this.
Erasing those recordings... is a cruel suggestion.
It's rational.
Hello, I'm Kristen, Control of Rhine Lab.
Nasti.
The talented Banshee, freshly recruited by Sterleigh Construction Co, who completes projects three times faster than regular engineers, provoking rumors of you imbuing witchcraft into blueprints...
We're currently establishing an engineering department at Rhine Lab. Might you be interested?
I happened to overhear your chat with the boss yesterday... 'Rhine Lab' is a bio-tech company, yes?
Look up, Nasti... Do you see those fowlbeasts, and the morning stars behind them?
I'm only looking for some kind of power to break through the sky.
The sky...
Per your prior suggestion, if you wish to accomplish something that exceeds the borders of common sense, then perhaps the power you need doesn't come from our side...
Rhine Lab should be looking into a scientific investigation department instead.
A rational suggestion. Can I take this as the first proposal fielded by our Director of Engineering?
Miss Lunorey?
I thought you'd send me straight to the hearing, or perhaps a more confidential cell... but here we are, waiting for a cable car at the ropeway station. Are we to visit the levitation layer next?
Your visitor has indeed arranged for that.
Long time no see, Nasti.
...So it was you.
I just got here, and what a fine place it is. I still have half the place to see—shall we have a look together?
Alright.
Why is it raining only in the area we just passed through?
Cloud projections, thanks to a sky simulator system developed by Wrankwood. Wait five minutes, and you can even enjoy an aurora.
A century ago, that fellow known today as the father of projections once lamented to me on a film set, wondering when we could actually shoot a film up in the sky...
'Sky Fever' has truly set the revolutionary gears of film tech in motion.
Quite an atmosphere here, Nasti. Personally, I take civilization's rapid developments as commonplace happenings, and now I can't help but be moved by the Columbians' creativity.
It's hard to believe you'd still be in the mood for fun and games.

According to the laws drawn up for Propeller Paradise, relevant authorities must call for a hearing within twelve hours of an emergency event happening to investigate the matter and notify the public.
Gordon barged into Rhine Lab's Vivarium, wrecked our research projects, and exposed his shady dealings to public scrutiny.
Evidence and testimonies provided were complete and accurate, your people arrived quickly, yet you've been dragging your feet to begin this hearing...
This is no procrastination, Nasti.
Two years ago, the Columbians never thought they'd fight the clouds for the right to dwell above. But now?
Propeller Paradise has become the hottest topic in all of Columbia, and its name tops the special plate registry records.
Take a moment to ride the cable car as it circles around. Look at the facilities around you, and at the company representatives clogging the entrance to the registration center...
All of this already tells us what sort of judgment the hearing will produce, no?
Propeller Paradise. Hmph. You get it? 'Paradise', representing a zealous ambition to fly high up into the skies, while 'Propeller' has an exact implication too—there's no stopping.

A smuggling network exposed, revealed to be led by the chief executive involving nearly a hundred companies, numerous irregularities found in the park's research work over the past six months... once we begin investigating, the exhibition will have to be canceled.
You're right, Nasti. The hearing must convene, and everything that's happened must be accounted for—
But that's all they need. An account.
And Rhine Lab is the most appropriate party to provide that?
After all, this 'sky fever' began that night in Trimounts. Anyone could believe Rhine Lab will do something like this again.
*sigh* Never the silent technician connecting nothing but cables that your peers say you are.
Though, this isn't exactly a false accusation against Rhine Lab now, is it?
Your mystery 'tree' still floats in the sky. Putting aside your 'botanical unit' and other excuses, how are you going to explain that?
It sounds like I don't even have to attend the hearing anymore. You've already made up your mind, after all.
...I'm sorry, Nasti.
I'm not sure of the exact timeframe, but I'll go home eventually. Someone's waiting for me.
Before that happens, Rhine Lab can become one of the stops on your journey.
It doesn't matter if you want to develop a new language, create a new Convallis, or change the so-called 'fate' of your clan... no one will get in the way of your goals.
On the contrary, Rhine Lab will fight for everything you need. After all, we have many like you.
And the catch? Or perhaps I should ask... the cost?
Nasti, I don't ask company employees to view Rhine Lab's operations as some sort of grand undertaking worthy of personal sacrifice.
If we want to accomplish the unprecedented, we are already on a path strewn with enough doubt and loss...
So, we must never stop. Ever.
...I consent to join Rhine Lab then.
Seeing as we're still on the cable car, I'm sure there's room for discussion.
Mm-hm?
The hearing must take place, and everything has to be accounted for, but it's merely an accounting...
All of Columbia knows about the Horizon Arc Project from two years ago. Kristen stole funding from the DOD, but Rhine Lab as a whole was not fully involved...though, I was certainly her accomplice.
Pinning the blame of endangering the entire park on a company versus an individual has entirely different implications, Nasti. Are you willing to sacrifice yourself like this for Rhine Lab?
Sacrifice? Kristen and I aren't fans of the word.
Everything has a price. This is what life in the Convallis and Kazdel taught me. There is no way I'll willingly take the blame for one of Maylander's plans.
I won't make it hard for the other Rhine Labbers.
Nor that 'tree', for that matter.
Do as you wish with the device, but let my assistant take the data, since the research isn't finished. He knows nothing about my project, so don't worry about him tampering with it.
We're here.
The agents at the station will escort you where you should go.
Right, there's something else. When I returned to Kazdel, I went to the Convallis...
Væla is here... I already know.
I had planned to hurry to find her before I went to the hearing.
(Væla is in the park...)
(Heh, Mercia. So I see...)
I'll send someone to look after my old friend.
Thanks.
The hearing on the sudden accident on Propeller Paradise has just concluded.
Suspect Nasti Lunorey abused her position as the head of the Park's Engineering Department to conduct unauthorized experimental projects, and this nearly led to a disaster.
On the night of the incident, the suspect attacked Gordon Tannen, the park's chief executive, in an attempt to cover up her crime with an unidentified tree-shaped device.
According to Mr. Tannen's testimony, the suspect not only deliberately tried to harm him, but she also abused her Engineering privileges to tamper with exhibits.
She forced her subordinates to conduct illegal tests, deliberately suppressed rival tech companies, and forced dozens of outstanding companies to leave the park.
Now, with the ATAO's backing, the innocent corporations implicated in this incident have returned to Propeller Paradise and will continue to contribute to Columbia's aerial operations.
The suspect Nasti Lunorey is currently in custody after confessing to her crimes. She will be sent to the D.C. courts to face justice.
From now on, Propeller Paradise operations will be overseen by Gordon Tannen and Gustave, supervised by the Maylander Emergency Management Council...
...The highly anticipated Air Expo Columbia will not be affected whatsoever.
Madam Væla... was that too loud? My apologies.
Nasti... what happened to her?
Don't you mind that, Madam.
Let your final memory of Director Lunorey be when you saw her last night.
The young agent places a hand on the old projector and gently turns its dials, as if manipulating delicate strings of memories.

Maylander Emergency Management Council intervenes in all Columbian incidents, in the name of monitoring the emergency measures of government agencies, for incidents produce the easiest facts to manipulate.
The Tin Man always jokes about how the Council's methods are more extensive than Mama John's stocklists, and this of course includes Mercia's Originium Arts—
Erasing memories.
Mercia has done the same actions hundreds, thousands of times. But now, her hand turning the dial, freezes up.
No... at the very least, I shouldn't do this to an old woman with already fading memories.
Besides, the facts have been tampered with enough today.
A late hearing, satisfied corporate reps, my fellow agents infiltrating the crowd... what exactly is the Tin Man's mission this time?
The more people on patrol, the less I can do for you—it's only a matter of time before he discovers my deception.
But if you can't stop worrying over Director Lunorey, I'll figure out a way...
Nasti... my daughter, I'm sorry.
It was I... who left you homeless.
'Where the torches are lit, the myriad souls be.'
'Bones to bones, souls to souls.'
Let's conclude the funeral here, Væla. Our people have returned to the courtyard, and night is falling.
Three months. Demoi spent three whole months in the wooden hut under the Roosting Tree before death finally came.
I've done all I could, but... I could not alleviate the pain of slowly turning into an empty husk.
She was never the sorrowful sort. The reed beds along the lake shore would sway to the sound of her laughter.
But every pleasant memory from the past two centuries vanished completely within the first three days after she moved into the wooden hut. I couldn't retrieve them, as much as I wanted to.
In those final three months, as her remaining memories abandoned her, all she could do was force out broken, hoarse whispers from her throat.
In her two centuries of life, she certainly knew laughter in full measure—at least.
But why did she have to end up meeting death in agony?
In these two hundred years since we settled in the Convallis... every Banshee that has left us, has experienced a long and painful period of losing her memory before death.
A cruel joke, simply put. To the departed, Banshees are the last ones to remember their existence. It is not in our nature to forget, but to remember.
Forgetting is indeed a new phenomenon of the last two centuries, but you shouldn't blame yourself for a chronic disease we have yet to comprehend.
A chronic disease, or a cost?
Every time, every person without fail, first loses her memories of life in the Convallis.
As if... the life we created was mere stuffing made of cotton—a substitute that replaced the life we were originally born to live, but in the end...
When it decays completely with time, the people it once filled are left as empty husks.
Is that not what we have always wanted to do?
To create a different life for the Banshees, the Sarkaz—a life accompanied by song and pastry, rather than war and death.

Remember what Her Highness promised us? As we deal with all of this in the Convallis, she, as the King of Sarkaz, will guide our people to bid farewell to our omnipresent vengeful and bloodthirsty nature.
You personally told me that Her Highness has turned to Originium for answers... along with that 'Dame'.
I had a few more conversations with Her Highness after that. She never forgot our original dream.
She only changed to a more decisive method to fulfill her promise. If the day comes where her flowers can cover the land... all the more we Sarkaz will need a new way of life.
I only hope the Convallis will hold on until then.
The Roosting Tree is still withering. Its roots have long since rotted. Neither the Children of Soil and Stone's witchcraft nor my own incantations have been of any effect.
I'm sure there's a future for our Roosting Tree. I only hope... you'll be there to see it.
These past three months, have you gotten even a single night's sleep as you kept Demoi company?
Her moments of peace were thanks to your soothing incantations, that she could gain respite from listening to the boats on Mist Lake.
But what about you?
Now that Demoi is gone, the only Banshee left who experienced the war two centuries ago are you and me.
Only I can pay the price.
What about the children?
Is that why you sent Aefanyl to Kazdel?
The Banshees' future should be left to the children.
Whether our decision was right or wrong, we shouldn't let the children pay for it.
Is that why you didn't let Nasti join in the funeral rites this time?
And good that I didn't. If the Convallis life is but a fragile existence made of cotton stuffing, then...
Nasti grew up fighting for her life in Kazdel; to her, death is nothing but Originium crystals sprouting all over the ground.
Her attitude towards death will spread doubts to the other children.
She has a bright mind. I'm sure she'll understand your intention.
I remember when she wanted to build a hut of her own by the Roosting Tree, though in the end you had her build it in the mistapple pulp woods.
You hardly bring her to the courtyard gatherings, nor do you allow her to formally study incantations and elegies... How do you explain all that to her?
I found the children too loud, so as the 'Custodian,' I had to move further away to find some peace and quiet. She saw how busy I was...
And she believed it?
At least, she said nothing.
Alright, Laqeramaline. You should go.
And you?
I wish to stay a while longer.
You've sung this elegy long enough.
...I don't know what else I can do. The Convallis is headed towards decay, and beyond it, the souls left homeless by my mistakes can only weep.
You can still go back and keep Nasti company at least.
As a mother, we might not be able to pay the cost of always shutting our children out.
You close the door only to hide cruel truths. But to the child, you're pushing her away...
These days, I've been getting the feeling that Nasti will leave...

lines.st: But I never thought the day would come so soon.

lines.x: Yes, it is an abrupt decision.
A flimsy boat drifts downstream out of the valley, propelled by an old Banshee's incantations.
Reeds sway as a thin mist veils the fallen leaves on the lake's surface. With the season indiscernible, the farewell is bereft of a seasonal undertone.
I had hoped for you to spend the rest of your life peacefully in the Convallis.
It was my mistake, not finding you earlier than I did. You'd already gone through so much suffering.
When I decided to bring you back here, I should have been responsible for you. But in the end...
Please forgive my conflicted heart, Nasti.
I'm not leaving because of you, Væla.
...At least, not for the reason you think.
Unlike those children, you know how Kazdel is, even now...
The living still fight in the market teeming with the stench of rust and excrement, while crystals sprout from the dead like untended weeds...
It does not hold the answer I want.
But what is your question in the first place?
Where are you planning to go?
I don't know. Maybe I'll use the half-baked incantations I picked up from you, cast a few stones at the valley's entrance, and let them decide a direction for me.
Leithanien? Laterano? Columbia?
I heard those strange names when wandering in Kazdel.
Those places...
They're very far...
So?
Take care, child. Eat well.
After a long silence, Væla only manages one sentence.
The old Banshee who has traveled far and wide bearing word of demise only realizes now, how unfamiliar those places sound to her.
Her adopted daughter is about to embark on a journey, yet she has no idea how it will end, or what sort of advice to impart.
As the boat gently glides by to the murmur of water, Nasti does not turn around.
Farewell... Mother.
Attention all company representatives and testers: Propeller Paradise will link up with D.C. in two hours.
The park's broadcast will begin once we're in range of the intercity net. Please make sure your exhibits are all functioning optimally. Air Expo Columbia is about to begin!

Look, that's Tower Mountains' finbeast airship! I heard they made a lot of last minute modifications and greatly improved its heat resistance.
And that solar-powered hover kitchen kit! With a power source that works even beyond the Starpod... I've been curious ever since I first read the brochure!
Whoa!
Good thing you only hit the window rail, instead of getting your whole head stuck in there.
The expo's about to begin. Aren't you nervous at all?
Relax. Professionals always have an escape plan ready. I'll get you out when the time's right.
You want to leave already?
N-No, sir! Of course not!
Whatever, I'm just here to hand over some stuff.
That's a lot of papers. Are these records and investigation forms? And the name on them—Ferne Barn?
Vandalizing public property, theft of cultural relics, trespassing, impersonating a guide... why's a little indie guide worth your trouble to scrutinize five years of work history?
We just need your signature down here, and you'll spend the rest of your life in jail introducing folks to handcuffs and shackles.
What do you want?
The expo's about to begin, but we need more hands on deck. We can't afford to lock up the park's only two guides, that would waste time and resources.
But there are too many records with your name on them, and you're connected to Nasti Lunorey.
So you've decided to threaten me?
Stay in line, and this is a chance to wipe your rap sheets clean, you know.
Seems like I don't have a choice.

Save your thanks for Gustave. He was the one who proposed it to the Maylander Emergency Management Council. The two of you would be missing out on this expo marking the beginning of a new era without him.

I'm sure you heard the broadcast too. Before the park plate drives into the intercity net's range and begins the live stream, make sure you're fully prepared to introduce the future to every Columbian out there.
I get it. I'll finish all the studio work, and won't leave the park before the expo ends.
Good deal.
Then come on out, you two. Time to get to work.
'On behalf of all Leithanians, I express my most sincere congratulations for the upcoming Air Expo Columbia to be held on the experimental Propeller Paradise.'
Take a guess, who signed it in the end?
Conductor of the Güldenes...gesatz, uh, Guardian of the Nine Kreise... anyway, one with a string of titles neither of us could pronounce—
The Kaiserin.
The late Maylander Sr. always said those with a keen sense for politics never lose it, and it seems that holds true.
Miss Wilson.
I'd rather keep a keen sense for wine-tasting, honestly.

Laterano took half a century to hold their Summit of Nations, an event you could say was the largest diplomatic event on Terra in nearly two centuries, yet Leithanien only sent over a few insignificant dukes as delegates.
But our tiny Air Expo Columbia has got a personal letter of congratulations from the Kaiserin...
Mr. Vice President, Propeller Paradise has sent over a gift. A bunch of balloons in the shape of the President, most of which are already decorating the hallways.
Thank you, Pavard.
Perhaps you could return the Kaiserin's wishes with a balloon like that one?
And let the Gesatzswächter and Spire Casters spend three days carefully dismantling it into rubber scrap?
It seems to be an average balloon for decorative purposes.

I fear that is no longer the case, since the letter was penned by the Kaiserin. It'll 'transform' into the most cutting-edge military tech—an aerial bomb disguised as a balloon, sent floating into another country's airspace.
And how many congratulations like that have you received?
Let's see. Victorian Grand Dukes, the Ursine Speaker, the Grand Knight of Kazimierz... I'll run out of fingers to count on.
The VP before me spent four years worrying about immigration and tariffs, but me?
Impregnable cities got captured, a never-changing sky got ripped open, and a long-silent ocean began to speak.
After all that, the core nations unanimously decided 'balloons are always aerial bombs in disguise'.
And what about us?
Oh, this balloon of ours can't even fly.
Before the balloons were sent over, the flammable hydrogen got replaced with regular air... for safety reasons, of course.
How safe and careful we Columbians are.
This sounds like a serious matter of national security, but you're discussing it with a retiree...?
Inside the State Building, the finest reception hall in all of D.C., even?
It's hard to imagine retirement making things any different for Jara Booker Wilson.
My hospitality is hardly befitting the once Eternally Shining Star, or Rhine Lab's former Director of HR.
These days, people seem more willing to be moved by a legend or miracle than by politics or promises.
Even the most rational congressmen are no exception.
If you're referring to the few meetings I had with Congressman Wayne, I believe you've already expressed your gratitude... with those unique souvenirs from Kjerag.
That was just a personal token of my gratitude. Besides, I later realized the scientists at the observation station must have sent you something similar before.
If you show up and give the right people the chance to put hydrogen back into Columbia's balloons, I'm sure I won't be the only one in line to thank you.
To my understanding, the most suitable people to do it would be those who made the balloon in the first place.
I've stepped in on their behalf quite a few times.
Researchers are as sincere as children. I can never bear to see their hopes dashed.
You're right that retirement has made no difference to me whatsoever, though.
Of course, we all know how important Rhine is to you.
Look. Propeller Paradise... you don't even need binoculars to see it anymore.
We need only wait and see what they'll turn Columbia's balloons into.
Look! This high-altitude adaptive balloon in the shape of the President is one of our hot souvenirs!
A thin film on the balloon's surface automatically adjusts its molecular structure according to the environment, so it won't explode, even if it collides with the Barrier.
Think about it, Miss! Just how many balloons could we fit under the Barrier?
I remember agents are required to pass a university-level physics fundamentals exam.
Isn't this park's purpose to push the boundaries of physics itself?
Are you implying those samples and snacks on you are for research purposes?
(Satisfied chewing)

Just for disguise purposes. I'm about to buy some fresh cotton candy planets over at platform four—a small vacuum chamber that can simulate the birth and development of Terra... don't you think it romantic?
At least, that's less boring than having to transport a special someone.
Special... you mean that Banshee?
Yeah, the one you were in charge of before.
So the Tin Man already knew...
He said you had an urgent mission to handle, so we'll take over evacuating the special individual.
Væla in bad shape. You'll have to be very careful.
I know, an old Banshee has to be skilled in witchcraft, no? We'll set up cognitive interference detectors to make sure nothing goes awry.
Cognitive interference? But she's already being tormented by her shattered memories—
Man, where my colleagues at? We have to evacuate all park personnel from the levitation layer in the next half hour, or I won't get to buy any souvenirs.
This is the last chance we'll get to see such weird stuff.
I think you should go buy your cotton candy planets. They won't be coming.
Huh? I—
I'm... at the ropeway station?
Right, I gotta go to platform four, or I'll miss out on the cotton candy.
Madam Væla, my apologies, but I only bought you thirty minutes.
Act on your desires. You're free.
The door, is open.
Guiding me... home?
...But where is... Nasti...?
There's still an hour before the broadcast begins. Which necktie should I wear...?
Tch. Is Gustave still deciding who will make the opening speech? Drag it out any longer and we'll have no time to prepare the script!

An hour won't be enough to list all my countless contributions to the park. There are also the many organizations and departments to thank for all the help they've given me, they deserve the recognition...
Everything will go as planned, surely...?
What am I thinking!? We may not get along, but there's no way he'll side with a Kuranta he's only known for a few days.
*sigh* Even at the very end, Gustave never changes. Using every trick up his sleeve to keep his lackeys in line, just to make them do as they're told.
We're like a bunch of kids. Getting all nervous over a little prize we deserve.
How come Five-Four's not back yet? Wasn't it supposed to just check the office for any news?
Who's that?!
...A letter?
I knew it... I knew it!
After hesitating for so long, he settles on the obvious choice, me!
How much effort did I put in just to snatch the opportunity to transfer here? How much did I have to finagle to climb the ladder from my lowly admin job in six months?
I'm the chief executive of Propeller Paradise, I'm the only one who can front it!
Oh, he even wrote a speech for me? That sly old fox, never leaves anything to chance.
'My fellow Columbians, I am Gordon Tannen, the chief executive of Propeller Paradise. I'm sure I've kept you waiting in front of your screens for some time now.'

'Now, it is with a heavy heart that I announce the official cancellation of Air Expo Columbia. A major accident occurred in the park, and as the one in charge of the place, I bear an undeniable responsibility for this...'
Wait, an accident? Is this a mistake? This is a—
A public apology?
Alert! Alert!

Patrol complete, letter not obtained. Part of the path has been blocked by a group of Terrans. Based on their travel route, they are presumably heading toward the levitation layer's experiment zone.
At the same time, detecting several high-powered Originium products on the move, bearing cargo identification tags...
They belong to Gordon Tannen.
My tags...?
An apology letter, and the news brought back by Five-Four... This is no mischievous prophecy, but indisputable reality.
Shock, panic, anger... countless emotions fill Gordon's chest.
Is Gustave about to cause an accident? On Propeller Paradise, the expo all of Columbia has been eagerly waiting to see?
For what reason? When did he begin planning this? What sort of pawn is 'Gordon Tannen' to him?
'Apology', 'Amends'—Gordon is all too familiar with these words. Ostracized by his colleagues and driven by his eccentric hobby, he was always 'violating the rules'.
The thin sheet of paper before him reflects the cumulation of his work over the past seven years.
He wrote hundreds of self-reflection letters during his time at ATAO. He remembers every single time Gustave would demand an apology from him, and how he slid from defiance to compromise.
The reward he deserved, the things he begged Gustave for like a child—were accusations against him all along.
Did you get the speech, Tannen?
So? Can you do it?
His carefully chosen necktie tightens, like a torture device.
All I have to do, is do as I'm told.
Platform 3 secondary patrol complete. Target pod in position, no suspicious individuals detected.
There's no one else left on this little platform but us. What's the sitch on your end?
The four pods' engines have undergone breakpoint testing, and the park's about to enter the intercity net's range.
Remember the official line to the public: The gathering is only temporary, and they can all return to their respective exhibit areas once the broadcast's intro segment is done.
Understood.
You sure everyone's gone on your end? The Tin Man stressed we had to make sure all park personnel are safely evacuated before we send the operation signal.

The main road was sealed off an hour ago, the support platform's emergency jump pads are all shut, and the cable car routes were all diverted to only go in one direction last night, so no one's been able to get in.
Better play it safe...
Ropeway station needs backup. That Rhine research assistant is blocking the access hub with a group of testers, and it's hard to get around them...
Gah, go back them up. It's crucial we time this right, every preparatory step has to be complete before the broadcast begins.
What was that...?
Look, over here.
A soda bottle cap?
Probably rolled off the table from the floating pod shaking. It's probably nothing, let's go.
Phew... M-Mr. Aspen...
What on earth is your obsession with that balloon?! The couch is small enough without you hanging onto that, I almost got squished flat!
I'm sorry, Miss Barn... but I begged you to take me here just for this balloon...
You have no idea how much effort it took slipping right out from under their noses and then getting you here!

Really sorry about that... could you hand me the toolbox from the closet? The evacuation announcement came so suddenly, I didn't have the time to make sure the floating pods would be alright during the stream...
You want the Lucky Cystybeast on display at the expo that badly?

Ignoring a public hearing, signing a temp contract with a tour guide with multiple charges under her belt, and helping her escape park security just so you can have her bring you back to a sealed-off levitation layer...
You guys! All nuts for the sky!
I'm drowning in debt here. The only way to clear it is to have the Lucky Cystybeast at the show and draw in investors.
Yeah, yeah. 'The sky will answer every future seeker', and all that jazz. Propeller Paradise's slogan is plastered all over the place, trashcan lids in the slums included.
Even if it's blind faith, I'm not going to give up now after coming this far.
Phew... nothing wrong with the components. They'll last till the show ends. Let's go.
Why's the door locked?
Wait, did you hear that?
I did... don't tell me something's really up with my product?
No, I don't mean the exhibit. From—
Under our feet?
Grr, why isn't the line moving? Drag this out any longer and the broadcast is gonna start!
Calm down, Maylander guys are on their way to handle it... they've got someone surrounded. Seems like a Rhine Lab researcher, judging by the uniform?
Let me through! The tree's hard drive and motherboard haven't been removed yet—
Don't push your luck!
Allowing the Rhine tree at the expo has been our biggest concession to Rhine Lab, and we've even been so generous as to let you copy the data for future research. What more do you want?
I just need five minutes! I promise I won't pull any tricks either! Hasn't the ATAO's Science Consultant issued a report on the tree? It's completely harmless!
No. All levitation layer personnel are to return to the ground to ensure the exhibits are finalized, and only return after the opening speech.
Besides, the uplink cable cars aren't operating now, so there's no way you can reach the Rhine tree. It's at the highest point in the park.
I didn't realize how much effort Director Lunorey put into this experiment until I saw the core code, and now I remember, how she never abandoned her dreams for the sky...
Even if it only saves a little backtracking time later, I want to help her somehow... you have to let me through!
Mr. Jagger, one more move and we'll have to resort to more special methods.
Wait... the tree looks strange.
You used the same excuse ten minutes ago.
No, no! It really looks weird! Look for yourself!

lines.st: The expo is about to open, with the Columbian sun shining brightly. The crowd follows Sky's finger, and spots a dazzling tree.

The branches wrapped in special material should have been completely still, like tiny insects trapped in resin. However, right here and now under the sunlight, the broken branches seem to glow... green.

lines.x: Organic materials don't photosynthesize...
The park handbook states clearly it is common for exhibits to transform in high-altitude environments.
Rhine stuff doesn't do that.
And it doesn't give you an excuse to go back up there.
Going up there won't solve the problem, I have no idea what exactly's going on...
But I know who does.
Huh? He just left, that easily?
...Levitation layer, eleven main platforms, and twenty support platforms have been inspected...
...Jammers and backup detonators have been installed in nine smaller levitation pods, which will crash one-by-one when activated...

...Personnel evacuation is complete. Scouts have done multiple calculations of the fallout curve based on current wind direction and force, and have confirmed falling debris will not reach the square serving as the evacuation spot...
All areas progressing well. Gustave sends word from the Cloud restaurant's cockpit that Propeller Paradise will be within D.C. intercity net range in five minutes.
Your orders, sir?
Anyone seen those two newbies from Bravo?
The ones responsible for that Banshee? Positioning shows they're still on Platform 3. That's not in the plan's operation range. Should we send someone to check it out?
Seems like their mission's been 'taken over'.
Platform 3 is far from the detonation target, so don't worry about that. I'll handle it later.
All teams, sync chronos. Phase two begins now.
The jail cell window crops the sky into a tiny square.
The ordinary yet mysterious 'tree', centerpiece of Nasti's research, and Rhine Lab's core project, is now hidden from her view by strangely clothed figures and floating balloons.

In a few minutes, the expo broadcast will begin, without Nasti's involvement. Her bone pen and mechanical devices were seized earlier, so all she needs to do now is to play the role of a helpless sinner.
If Muelsyse were here, she'd probably summon eight water clones to scold her for being silly. But Nasti does not regret her decision, though there is one woman on her mind.
Væla leaving the Convallis had always left her uneasy. If the chance came...
Who's that?
Director Lunorey, you have to follow me to the tree platform!
You're supposed to be keeping the device data and core code safe. What are you doing here?
But the 'tree' is growing now!
...'Growing'? That's impossible.
So that's not supposed to happen... If it affects the levitation module, it'll definitely be dangerous to anyone on the ground.

The levitation module runs independently, with a backup power supply that can last for five days. But the abnormality you mentioned... the only way to find out why it's happening is to go inside the 'tree'.
Come with me, Director.
The security cams have been rigged with a delayed feed that should buy us at least twenty minutes, and I'm not sure why, but the cell's smart locks are unlocked, so now's—
That's because the chief executive of 'Propeller Paradise' opened them for you.
Gordon?
What's... all that on you? A DIY high-altitude face shield, modified propellers, and a second-hand insulated spacesuit?
Did you loot them from the park's scrap and recycle plant?
Your distorted testimony at the hearing is still up on the park's bulletin board, but now you're helping us escape...? Don't tell me you've had a sudden attack of conscience?
Nasti, I got your bone pen and devices here. Sorry about the hearing.
I'd rather stand in the defendant's dock with you than recite that prepared speech—after all, the confiscated devices were my goods anyways, so I'll take the blame for that.
...Who are you and what have you done with Gordon Tannen?
What happened?
My dear citizens of D.C., and those watching this live broadcast, it is my honor to introduce Propeller Paradise, an experimental park that is sure to leave its mark on Columbian history!
We don't have much time.
Nasti, you've done deals with Gustave. I'm sure you won't doubt the intel I'm about to hand over.
Your old boss is planning something with Maylander, correct?
You're even sharper than I thought.
Seeing the park's chief executive here when he's supposed to be making a speech in half an hour is proof enough.
Friends, look out your windows, up at the sky. No amount of marketing bluster can compare to what you'll see with your own eyes, where the future among the clouds is waiting for you!
They want to can the expo with an accident that'll kill the future of Propeller Paradise, though I've no idea exactly what their plan is.
If Maylander's involved, they'll move fast.
What was that?
God, am I seeing things? Something's falling from the levitation layer—Is that the Lucky Cystybeast's floating pod?!
No, there's more! Three... four... Is this part of the opening ceremony? They didn't mention this in the script! If they really come crashing down—
Wait! The pods stopped falling? As if something's gripping them firmly...
Huh? What was that? Oh, oh! It seems the tech companies have prepared a surprise that even got me!
We have just witnessed Propeller Paradise's breakthrough in the field of levitation, with no need for papers or data, and this is only just the beginning!
No way, there's no emergency stop module—even in ongoing projects—that could've done that...
Sky, your field measurements are more precise. Where were the falling pods supposed to land?
From visual estimates, they would have probably crashed into Platform 2, blocking the connecting lower layer tunnel—
That's the only path we can take to the tree.
Gordon, I accept your apology, but I have no time for your plans.
That miracle was no stroke of luck. Might have something to do with you and your invention?
Is that a threat?
Just my intuitive judgment on all rule-breaking.
I don't need your help with Gustave, but I do need a bargaining chip to make sure Propeller Paradise stays afloat when everything's done and dusted.
So my tree...
Letting you handle something only you know how to handle is part of my plan.
Got it. Rhine Lab staff will cooperate with you.
However. Whatever it is you're planning to do, go to the tree at the first sign of danger and I'll come get you.
Everything ready, Five-Four?
Preparations complete.
Let's go. I have an opening speech to make.
What's going on? The jammer and our backup detonators aren't working?
The transmitted signals show the devices are all activated, but the nine target pods' core engines have stopped.
Somehow, the received signals are all messed up. Even pods without transmitters attached are sending signals...
Get a team to investigate this. Have them remove all traces of our work from earlier, and retrieve the transmitters.
Captain... that's equivalent to calling the operation a failure.
You forget how we do things at Maylander? If reality happens to significantly deviate from our plan, cease all action immediately. We're not some Wrankwood espionage flick heroes trying to be tough.
Seems like someone is biased against blockbuster hits.
We don't get reshoots.
It's not as if we have no other choice.
Don't send anyone to the pods. It's probably the Banshee's incantation that's keeping those 'toys' from falling straight down.
But Nasti should be in jail right now, right?
She's not the Banshee I'm referring to.
The incantation has fundamentally changed how the levitation layer facilities are connected. The malfunctioning receivers prove this.
...You're telling me the Banshee's incantation is able to connect to the levitation layer's power supply structure, send signals and synchronize commands? That's the craziest thing I've ever heard!
Nasti designed the entire park's conduits and systems herself, after all. She didn't become one of the Rhine Lab Ten solely on her engineering talent.
Though, if we utilize the upper level's power grid, and detonate just one engine, that will cause the entire layer to fall.
Th-The entire layer...?
So you mean...?
The park will connect with D.C. in twenty-five minutes. We'll have to present a reverse firework display to Propeller Paradise.

Change of plans. Those on the upper layer, mobilize personnel and connect the support platforms surrounding the levitation layer to the common power grid; those on the lower layer, calculate the crash zone and prepare for evacuation measures at any moment.
I'll let Gustave know of this, and have the admin staff coordinate with us to delay the opening speech.

Also, send a group to the jail to confirm Nasti's position, and deploy at least three elite squads to the elevator square and any ascendable routes—it's only a matter of time before she returns to the levitation layer.
Are you not going to join?
Oh, I have to go supervise an agent of mine. Maylander has quite the troublesome evaluation systems after all. I'll join the rest of you once I'm done.
Alright teams, move out.
A giant broadcast screen hangs in the Cloud restaurant, incessantly switching between gaudy company logos and the newest levitation devices, while Oakley's agitated voice booms from the speakers.
The lower left of the screen is a real-time feed from the control room. A small red dot representing Propeller Paradise flashes, heading for Max, D.C.
Two cameras stand on a cloud-like carpet, pointing at a lone chair—like a defendant's dock awaiting Gordon Tannen's attendance.
You're late.
Why did you come dressed like that? There's no time to prepare a new outfit. We only have twenty minutes until your speech.
The tie on my ATAO uniform was too tight.
Heh. I remember six years back when you just got that set of clothes after your promotion to a full-time role. I personally adjusted it for you. So giddy from joy, you were.
You told me I had a lot to learn to be worthy of that uniform. And then I stayed in the same entry-level role for the next six years.
No newbie's first skill is writing self-reflection letters. It's not that I've never regretted helping you into that uniform.
And what about now?
This outfit, the blame for causing the accident—who put them on me?
Oh, so you've figured it out?
You seized two crates of my goods the night I was exposed. I thought you were helping me dispose of them, but I found out they ended up in the hands of you and Maylander.
I bet this apology speech wasn't written that night either, was it, sir?
Go on.
Importing Originium devices into Columbia clearly violates ATAO regulations, and if security is compromised, I'll be the sin eater for the whole park.
So you still think this is a personal grudge?
Am I wrong? I've always been a slum boy to you, a bad little rule-breaker. As I climbed through the ranks on my own effort, you only came to hate me more...

Your own effort? Do you honestly think a useless bum with disciplinary records and hundreds of self-reflection letters on file could manage a transfer to the highly coveted levitating experimental park on his own?
What...?
You disappoint me, honestly. Even now you still have only a superficial understanding of our current situation.
Your title, investments, achievements, and results from the very moment you stepped onto Propeller Paradise were all 'gifts' from me to you.
Gordon Tannen, have you ever wondered how you manage to procure foreign Originium products so easily?
You knew about my dealings from the start...
Even your stock lists were meticulously picked by my hand.
The trident Arts units from Leithanien are secret equipment used by the Gesatzswächter for military training.
The power shields used by Kazimierzian campaign knights were disassembled into three parts, and you put them into the exhibits of three different companies.
And the Originium engines and Arts cores sourced from foreign military equipment? If the park blows up because of them...
You aren't the brightest bulb on the chandelier, but you have a good eye. Propeller Paradise is indeed a giant testing ground, but it's not testing those toys in the sky.
Give it about ten more minutes, and the 'sky fever' you helped ignite will burn into Columbian flames of fury for 'foreign spies'.

The countless exhibits in the sky with their core technologies inside them? They are all military reserves for countering our foreign foes. Naturally, Propeller Paradise will become a base for arms testing.
Military...? I thought you would've considered ATAO's interests, at least.
'We'll always be of one mind when we stand for Columbia.'—What matters is our country, man.
So, this apology speech is your stepping stone to a new role. I might even be able to pull some strings and get you a new role at the Department of Defense.
Why all the honesty? Not worried I'll sabotage the speech?
The fact that you've shown up here with the speech in hand means you've already made the wise choice.
To be honest, I wasn't sure you would make a qualified piece on the board. But now? That little monster beside you has answered that question for me.
Same as the rich love their cigars and watches. Simply enjoying the sense of superiority with their little hobbies.
...Five-Four isn't a toy made to cater to sky fever.
Sure, but you're no longer the reckless young man only concerned with invention and modification.
ATAO sanded down your edges, educated you in the ways of the land, made you think to operate within the 'rules' you set yourself—it made you like me.
Go, get your little monster ready too. Let's have it join your public apology.
It's a fitting symbol of what Propeller Paradise represents—gimmicky, kitschy, useless. An absolute waste.
Altitude readings are stable, and the pods show no sign of falling any further, while the engines aren't making any strange sounds... We should be safe now, right?
Fifty meters... Lucky Cystybeast's pod fell fifty meters...
What's that, Aspen?
Six months ago, I was at the crowded registration center going through the paperwork. I was first in line, and chose the most conspicuous levitation pod for my Lucky Cystybeast.
The closer you are to the Starpod, the more luck you get. That's what everyone in the park thinks, even if your pod is only a few inches higher than your neighbor's.
I lined up overnight just to get a spot closer to the sky, I hired over a hundred testers to secure an exhibit spot, and got smuggled contraband just to keep my Lucky Cystybeast running...
Miss Barn, I can tell you're no fan of the competition, but this is the basic law of Propeller Paradise. I'm sure you've seen it yourself on your tours.
I get a list of companies and corporate data updated daily, and there are people who just complain about their terrible lives only to head right back to their misery.
You only got here a month ago. To you, this is a place you just happened to visit. To us, it's our only path to a future, and only a few will survive.
Maybe you think I'm just a desperate, sick man talking out of his ass.
But when the levitation platforms rise alongside the thousands of pods, we just feel the excitement coursing through our veins for no reason, don't we?
I poured everything I had into the Lucky Cystybeast, and that's why I have to win this gamble.
I knew it. Even though the incantation can link multiple platforms and pods through the power lines, its range is still limited when calculated outward from the tree.
Barian, send a signal to Sky's team. Tell them we've found the pattern of the power connections.
Barian—
Ugh...
The scientific mind truly is brilliant, don't you agree? Thanks for helping us locate our mission objective.

Inform our engineering experts to stop their research. Centering on the platform beneath my feet, have the personnel stationed at the perimeter immediately cut all backup power and attempt to connect the supply lines through alternative methods.
Also, it is highly probable the Rhine Labbers have discovered our plan. Two researchers were found on Platform 4, but have been dealt with.
All patrols, speed up. Report anyone you see ASAP. Your colleagues in the broadcasting studio will buy us time by switching the camera feeds.
Act fast.
*huff*... *gasp*...
Those agents are effing persistent... Can those Maylander guys fly or something? How are they everywhere?
Good thing we lured them away, and there's no one here...
No!
Five testers and two Rhine Labbers found on support Platform 17. They have all been dealt with, and will be sent to detention on the lower level five minutes from now.
The operation is going smoothly. They lack the numbers, and are scattered like headless houseflies.
Well, everyone excels in their own fields, and they lack communication equipment or anyone to direct them.
What about Nasti?
She escaped from the lower-level cells ten minutes ago, but no contact has been noted between her and any of her staff.
She knows the park structure. We're at a disadvantage with such a tight time window.
But hide and seek is not a single-player game.
We'll continue our patrols. A smattering of testers and researchers will not significantly affect our operation.
Stay wary, and report back promptly.
Wasn't there one more person here just now?
Phew, he didn't notice me... Thanks for pulling me back, Mr. Jagger.
You're welcome. You good?
Truth be told, seeing the others knocked down so easily still has my knees feeling like spaghetti.
Can you run?
I can.
Let's split up here then. No time to slow down.
I'm here, sir.
(Wipes pipe) Let me have a smoke first, Merry.
You can look at those screens while I'm at it.

Mercia fixes her gaze at the dozen or so monitors on the wall, and sees the high-altitude elevator, the floating platforms, the ropeway station—almost every inch of Propeller Paradise is displayed on the screens.
The bright and bizarre structures in the sky seem to pop out from the screens, a strange sight in the dark room. As if Mercia was attending a post-modern art exhibition instead.
This place...
What of it?
...Nothing. I just didn't think Propeller Paradise would have such a... sealed off space.
Hmm? Reminds you of our interrogation room?
So, may I know why you were looking for me?

Look at the upper right monitor. You heard the explosion earlier, didn't you? The nearly ten floating pods connected to that Rhine Lab 'tree' should have been smoking heaps of scrap metal in the wastes by now.
Merry, I'm sure you know... what sort of force keeps them in the air still.
An incantation. I kept this secret from you, sir.

*sigh* Our colleagues at the Emergency Management Council have only just captured the dangerous criminals responsible for the explosion, and now they have to clean up the mess caused by their own people's negligence.
You've seen it. The Banshee's incantation has changed the way the levitation layer connects to the other platforms and pods. Any other incident happens, and it'll affect more than just nine pods.
The situation is dire.
Sir...
I shouldn't be speaking to you like this as a secretary, or a Maylander agent. But I understand my colleagues, and I know how the Council works.
Just now, I was looking at the surveillance footage, and they were busy munching on cotton candy. Inefficient methods of deception like that should be forbidden on such serious occasions.
I've completed my mission on Propeller Paradise, both monitoring Mr. Tannen and the many tech companies, and exposing their secrets. But now I see...
These missions were never to ensure the expo ran smoothly.
Be it the secret from the start, the explosion just now, or the so-called 'other incident' you mentioned... they've all been part of your plan, yes?
Haha. You're keen. You pass.
So what exactly...
Let's sit down before we continue.

lines.n: Big Larry & Ol' Curly & Lil' Mo
lines.x: Sit down sit down.
Come on, you three. Some manners would be good.
Is this a Maylander trial for a traitor, or...?
Or have I finally found time to handle a junior's troublesome evaluation process?
Don't worry. I'm just an old fart who wishes to impart some wisdom to the younger generation.
I'm curious what that wisdom is.
Oh, Merry. I'm sure you'll hate this rotten nonsense.
One year ago, Rhine Lab submitted a proposal to D.C., a project developing tech from Galleria Stellaria, and Congressman Street was inspired to expand the project for the whole country.
His secretary burned the midnight oil to prepare a slide show, showcasing what the congressman saw in his dreams after having seen the sky over Trimounts being torn apart...
(Holding and biting on Mercia's hat) Foundation stones built on the clouds!
(Walking while twirling Mercia's bow) Rain or shine, it'll all be under our feet!
(Swinging mid-air while holding onto Mercia's hair) Tour groups leaving the Starpod, one-by-one, as the twin moons are covered with Columbian flags!
And so on and so on. Congress voted. How did that turn out?
Two hundred and seventy-three to sixty-five. More than eighty percent voted yea. Hah.
And the government is eager to water and fertilize these fantasies.
I'm sure you know better than I do just how many companies and testers were scrambling to get into Propeller Paradise at any cost after that, Merry.
Too many to count.
Nearly half the companies in Columbia have added a sky-related department, and even air fryer manufacturers have been trying to add 'clouds' to applicable product lines.
Tin Man, don't forget about the stocks!
There are over two hundred sub-categories of sky-related concept stocks, with the longest registered validity period extending three hundred years into the future, hahaha~!
Companies willingly apply for loans with interest rates several times higher than the prevailing market rate just to secure a spot on Propeller Paradise...
I remember looking through Gordon's case files, and coming across a certain Aspen who bought contraband goods from him, just to clear inspections.

Now, it wasn't because he didn't know the law. It was because he was divorced, had sold his family assets, and was burdened with a hopeless mountain of debt. The so-called Lucky Cystybeast is a high-stakes gamble that will decide the rest of his life.
What a shame.
...A shame?
He used to run a grocery store in a town by the shipping routes near D.C., and even now there might be some pioneers out there still using the tools he peddled.
But here he is with this invention, that floating revolving bar where 'the people turn, but not the beer'... What else can you even do with that? Other than invite your foes for a drink?
Simply put, what he's done is akin to a student frantically writing compliments in the margins, hoping the teacher will give him a perfect score.
Take the future as a real test. Who's going to be the examiner? You? Me? The generals at the DOD? Or maybe Congress?
Maybe it'll be Kazimierz, who just equipped all their campaign knights with power armor? Or Leithanien, with their recent addition of a 'Basic Arts Tactics' course to all school curricula?
Or perhaps Ursus, with its growing army, their mighty force that's left their mines empty? Could it be Ægir, currently shelling the Iberian coast with artillery along with the Sea Monsters?
Among these parties, I fear it is the Sea Monsters who are the most likely to visit a floating revolving bar.
Sir, you mean to say that the Columbians' fanaticism for the sky...
Is completely meaningless?
Compared to what's happening everywhere else right now, I'm afraid so.
Columbia has always been driven by fanaticism. I'm sure you're crystal clear on this, given your background, Mercia.
Without a fanatical drive for freedom, we wouldn't have had our frontier rebels eighty-plus years ago, and we Columbians would still be living under the Victorians' heel.
Without a lust for wealth, our mass pioneer movement would not have happened, and nearly half of Columbian land today would remain an untraversable wilderness even for the strongest burdenbeasts.
But this time... it's just a waste.
So you think Propeller Paradise should take the blame?
Why would I? It has already accomplished its mission perfectly... well, it's one step away.
It's stirred up the Columbians' zeal more effectively than I could've ever hoped to. Now, it just needs to 'redirect' it.
Through its ruin?
Through it 'being destroyed'.
By who? Who else in this park could...?
Wait. Mr. Tannen's goods, smuggled from all across Terra... Kazimierz, Leithanien, Sargon, and even Kazdel...
They'll become weapons planted by foreign spies, deliberately intended to ruin Columbia's display of achievements and destroy our people's fantasies of taking to the skies.
Eighty-plus years later, the Columbians' rage will be reignited once more.
Kazimierzians might not want to see their tax money turn into knight armor, while Leithanian children might just run away from boring 'Basic Arts Tactics' classes.
But Columbians, we have our fervor. As we move to the future, we move forward together, tens of millions strong.
As long as we can direct this zeal onto a proper path.
'Collate our wills, and seize the truth of everything'...
Sir, I remember you telling me your story... I thought you always hated war.
It's been tens of thousands of years. The number of lost souls in my hand is no less than the casualties of a military campaign.
The reason I didn't burn in Kazdel's furnace then is that I hate meaningless sacrifices.
But this time... an arms race might just be the beginning. You and I know it won't end there, just like how the Great Expansion didn't end with the discovery of some ore.
So, the sacrifices Columbia needs for this competition won't be enough with just this park... correct?
I told you before, right? This is a whole bunch of rotten nonsense.
The Maylander Foundation didn't have to explain all of this to you, my dear agent. But the day will come when you'll have to accept the truth, even if you don't agree with it.
Don't forget your family name, Mercia... You're a Selene.
This is...

The elevators connecting the park's upper and lower levels have been sealed off, and the only ropeway station leading to the 'tree' is also out of service. Nasti has exhausted herself just by getting here.
Floating pods by her side are riddled with cracks, black smoke billowing from within. A sign their core energy modules have been destroyed, and they should have all crashed to the ground.
But the pods stubbornly remain amongst the clouds, piecing together a precarious path, as if an invisible pair of hands are holding them up.
Nasti?
Update the others. I'll go get her.
The agent rushes towards her, but just as he takes a step forward, the bricks under his feet shift.
Ah—
Target sighted. Location: Flying Donut station. Requesting backup.
We tried to intercept her, but something's up here. We—AH—!
...(Speaks in Sarkaz) Disorder?
Nasti recalls the river at the mouth of the Convallis of the Banshees. Still and calm, where even a child could safely row a boat on their own.
However, unassisted intruders find the dense forests on both sides constantly shifting positions, like a mobile labyrinth, with the distant riverbank always hidden beyond the thick mist.
After a momentary silence, Nasti steps out of the pod and into the empty sky. Brick and stone immediately form a floor beneath her.
(Speaks in Sarkaz) Disorder.
A path held together by an incantation.
Convallis Banshees often isolate themselves from outsiders, or decorate their own courtyards with this method.
She also used the same tactic to momentarily stall Saria back in the Ring Lab.
...This is a path specially paved, just for her.
