Hmph. She went back to Site 359 right after the inquiry. She said she had a few dreams... girl never looks forward.
Saria. Nasti's already sent the experiment results concerning the 'tree' to my mailbox. We're not here only to discuss the Propeller Paradise matter, are we?
Of course not.
Then who are we waiting for?
Perhaps you might be interested in seeing data from the Infy Icefield?
You're back, Maryam. Finally.
Sorry, it's been a long time since I've been in Columbia. Took me some time to fit my hoof-irons. Broke them in on the walk from D.C. to Trimounts.
I'm glad I didn't wait for you on Propeller Paradise. I had a car that could have taken both of us back to Rhine together.
...You know, I never considered that at all.
Someone obsessed with searching for death in every corner of Terra would be used to working alone. I figured.
It takes two people with director-level access to activate the core unit of that tree. I had wondered who Saria sent to help Nasti...
The legend is already a pile of bones in the wilderness. The media questions if the director of Rhine's Scientific Investigation Section even exists. There's definitely no better choice, but—
You want to know who arranged it? Justin Jr.
He had some investments in Čappat, and no sooner had I left the Infy Icefield than he got in touch.
He told me to go to Columbia, and straight to D.C.. I got Gustave's invitation soon after... Justin Jr. had obviously, let's just say, pulled a few strings.
Then why did you—
Why did I come back to Rhine? I found a fragment of that spherical superstructure in the depths of the Infy Icefield, where the rootless flowers bloomed the brightest.
You guessed it? Yes, it's the structure that Kristen's files mentioned. She said people in the old times called it the Stargate.
I'm sure Saria will share the information with you soon. Any more questions, Ferdinand? I thought not.
Haha. Nothing's changed about how you ask and answer your own questions, Maryam. You've left Ferdinand with nothing left to say.
Alright, looks like everyone's here.
This meeting is rather overdue. For the past two years, all of mainstream Columbian media and the tech tabloids have made the same judgment...

'The gap where Galleria Stellaria disappeared is a scar upon the Starpod, and a scar upon Rhine.' They've even come up with a whole narrative on the cancellation of Rhine's end-of-year cocktail party.
We were all busy, that's all.
Yes, but now we finally have some time.
The joint project between Engineering and Ecological had some unexpected twists and turns, but the final result was a smashing success. Nasti, you're now holding the key to a brand new domain.
...If we can keep it in our hands.
Maryam's discovery in the Infy Icefield has proven beyond all doubt that those strange tales that Kristen collected are, indeed, based on actual fact.
Perhaps it will help us rebuild dialogue with the old era.
And Muelsyse has brought back data from our observation station in Kjerag...
Data that's very interesting... or very dangerous.
I'm sure you've heard about the events in Bolívar. Twenty science teams have gone missing in the Black Flow.
Explorers and schemers will be excited if there are any developments with that swamp. Rhine should act too, Saria.
Magallan is already on her way.
The events and phenomena that have unfolded since Kristen's incident indicate that the most well-hidden secrets of Terra all lead to the same answer.
It will tell us the reason for Terra's existence, and whether it is a disaster or a brave new 'world' waiting for us in the future.
Rhine Lab has already made preparations, but that alone is not enough. We must respond to the fear of the unknown, and go boldly where no one has gone before...
What you said was absolutely correct, Nasti.
However, Rhine Lab must be the one to turn that key.
(Mutters) Whoa...
Of course, we'll have a make-up cocktail party.
What's this, Justin Jr.?
A newspaper sample. I would like you to think of a title for this article here before it goes to print. Are these scientists gathered to 'Mourn the End of Sky Fever'...?
Or preparing to 'Usher in a Tide of New Changes?'
You sent a screenshot of your communications with your colleagues to the The Savior?
More appropriate to put it as a DOD-aligned newspaper getting its hands on certain documents that could not be obtained by official means.
But I heard your Control has already reached an understanding with Jackson concerning Propeller Paradise.
None too thrilled about it, are you? But it won't stop me from making another deal with you.

You understand that, in the aftermath of the Horizon Arc Project, Rhine Lab's credibility with the Department of Defense has become roughly the equivalent of Mama John's scratch-off lottery tickets—garbage.
Which is why I only came to you after Saria reached an understanding with Jackson.
In the aftermath of the setback in Bolívar, the failure of the Horizon Arc Project, and the Propeller Paradise incident, the Department of Defense's credibility with President Mark Max has become...
Well, I suppose I should spare you the proverbs.
I don't know what exactly our scientists are planning, but I know the value they can create. In this, I'm sure we're in agreement.
I'm a businessman, and my priority is Rhine's business interests.
Sounds like I have no reason to refuse.
It's a pleasure doing business with you.
To be frank with you, I didn't think much of you when Blake recommended you...
Hah. I'm sure you remember my life story that I just shared, which is a fairly interesting one, if I do say so myself...
All you need to do is think something of fate. As for your attitude toward the one who controls its pulse—
That's rarely in your hands.
